.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:373:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer opening the document graph, I want a structured integrity panel that groups real document problems by kind and points me at the files to fix, so I can triage a noisy graph quickly and confirm when it is healthy.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. `docgraph.Graph.Warnings` (or a sibling `Issues` field) is a typed slice with `Kind`, `Path`, `ID`, `Message`, and optional `RelatedPath`. Kinds include at minimum: `duplicate_id`, `parse_error`, `missing_dep`, `id_path_missing`, `id_path_mismatch`, `required_root_missing`, `cascade_unknown`.
2. GraphQL schema exposes `DocGraph.issues: [GraphIssue!]!` with the same fields. The existing string `warnings` either stays (rendered from issues) or is deprecated with a schema comment.
3. Unit tests in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go` cover each issue kind: fixtures that produce exactly one instance of that kind and assert the typed output.
4. `/graph` page renders an **Integrity** panel when `issues.length > 0`:
   - grouped by `kind` with a count per group;
   - each row shows path, id, and message;
   - path links to the document viewer (reuses existing documents route);
   - total-issues badge appears next to the page title.
5. With zero issues, neither the panel nor the badge renders, and the graph view is visually identical to a healthy graph today.
6. Duplicate-ID rows show both paths and a "copy suggested unique ID" button; the suggestion is deterministic (same input → same output) and a unit test asserts the suggestion function's stability.
7. `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`) CLI command prints the same issue list grouped by kind, exits 0 if empty and 1 if any issues present. Integration test asserts exit codes and grouped output.
8. Playwright e2e:
   - navigates to `/graph` on a fixture repo with one seeded duplicate-id pair and one missing-dep target;
   - asserts the Integrity panel shows groups "Duplicate ID (1)" and "Missing dep target (1)";
   - expands the Duplicate ID group and asserts both paths are visible;
   - clicks the first path and asserts navigation to the documents route for that file;
   - on a clean fixture repo, asserts neither the panel nor the badge is present.

**Dependency:** best sequenced after `ddx-12cae4dd` lands so the fixture repos are not polluted by `.claude/worktrees` noise, but implementation does not block on it — the two can proceed in parallel and this bead's tests use synthetic fixtures.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-23T04:15:59Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3704155","closing_commit_sha":"8b291ee475105211ea3b3369e7bf0e88c82166a8","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:09:46.473146653Z","description":"## Observed

The graph view at `/nodes/.../projects/.../graph` renders a long flat list of amber-styled warnings, currently dominated by `duplicate document id "AC-AGENT-001" in "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.claude/worktrees/agent-a2818c5c/docs/resources/agent-harness-ac.md"` and similar entries. Two distinct concerns:

1. **Input noise** — most duplicates today come from `.claude/worktrees/<agent>/...` copies of the repo being indexed (tracked in `ddx-12cae4dd`). That fix will eliminate a large class of false positives but will not make the warning surface usable.
2. **Brittle degradation** — when documents have real problems (duplicate IDs in the canonical tree, broken frontmatter, missing `depends_on` targets, `id_to_path` mismatches), the system doesn't just warn — downstream consumers (documents list, staleness, UI routing) misbehave. And there is no workflow for a user to triage, diff, or fix the offending files.

## Current state of warnings

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/+page.svelte:22-30` dumps `graph.warnings: string[]` into a flat amber block. No grouping, no categorization, no links to the offending file, no way to dismiss or act.
- Warning strings are generated in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go` (duplicate id, parse failure, required-root-missing, id_to_path mismatch/missing, cascade unknowns) as unstructured `fmt.Sprintf` output.
- No stale-document-pruning: if a `.md` is deleted on disk, any id_to_path entry pointing at it yields a warning instead of being auto-cleaned.

## Scope of this bead (deliberately not overlapping ddx-12cae4dd)

Assume `.claude/worktrees` noise is gone. This bead delivers the **integrity surface** that remains valuable: a way to see, categorize, and act on real document problems.

### Deliverables

1. **Structured warnings** — extend `docgraph.Graph.Warnings` from `[]string` to `[]GraphIssue{kind, path, id, message, relatedPath?}` and plumb through GraphQL. Keep a `messageLines(): []string` shim for any caller still using the old shape during the grace period.
2. **Integrity panel on /graph** — replace the flat amber block with a collapsible panel grouped by issue kind (Duplicate ID, Missing dep target, Broken id_to_path, Parse error, Required root missing). Each group shows count + expandable list; each row shows file path (clickable → document viewer), the offending ID, and a copyable message.
3. **Summary badge** — small badge next to the page title showing total issue count, so a clean graph reads clean.
4. **Audit GraphQL query** — `query { docGraphIssues { kind, path, id, message, relatedPath } }` usable independently of the full graph for future dashboard use. Reuses the same structured slice.
5. **Repair affordances (minimum viable)** — for two common cases:
   - Duplicate ID: show both file paths side-by-side and a "copy suggested unique ID" button (deterministic suggestion, e.g., append a short hash of the path). No auto-write.
   - Missing dep target: show the document declaring the missing dep, and a copy-to-clipboard snippet of the frontmatter line to remove. No auto-write.
   Auto-repair is explicitly out of scope until we trust the detection.
6. **CLI parity** — `ddx doctor` (or `ddx docs audit`) prints the same structured issues in a terminal-friendly form, so headless users and CI can surface problems.

### Out of scope

- Fixing `.claude/worktrees` indexing (owned by `ddx-12cae4dd`).
- Auto-rewrite of source files.
- Cross-project graph integrity (today's graph is per-project).","events_attachment":"ddx-6ff597ca/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-23T04:26:29.781059658Z","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model "minimax/minimax-m2.7" for harness "gemini"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","feature":"FEAT-007","id":"ddx-6ff597ca","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-007","feat-008","ui","docgraph"],"notes":"**AC 7 — command path**: The command is registered as `ddx doc audit` (`cli/cmd/doc.go:59`), but the acceptance criteria specifies `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`). If `doc` is the existing noun, this is arguably fine, but the AC should be reconciled. The `Aliases: []string{"integrity"}` alias doesn't cover the spec'd names.
**AC 7 — `--json` exit code**: `cli/cmd/doc.go:79-83` — when `--json` is set, the command encodes issues and returns `nil` unconditionally. The AC requires exit 1 when issues are present regardless of output format, so CI pipelines using `ddx doc audit --json` would incorrectly report success on a broken graph. The test `TestDocAuditCommand_JSONOutput` even documents this as intentional ("--json always succeeds (exit 0) so piping into jq works"), which contradicts the AC. Either the AC should be amended or the `--json` path should also return `ExitError` when issues exist (with a `--exit-zero` escape hatch for pipe-friendly use).

`cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/graph.spec.ts:177` — AC 8 asks for a Playwright e2e on a fixture repo with one seeded duplicate-id pair and one missing-dep target. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:399:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T06:24:35Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604f17d53ce26b5776472f9fa77a82a2eb1b9cba","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:35:08.058474818Z","description":"The ddx-bead skill guides bead creation but underspecifies what makes a bead *execution-ready* for automated execute-bead runs. Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop driving: beads that technically met the current skill's requirements (title, AC, labels, description pointing at spec-id) still produced bad execute-bead outcomes because the description lacked the context the agent needed to ship correct code in one pass.

## Gaps in the current skill

1. **Description quality is underspecified.** The skill gives AC quality rules but the description field just says 'per SD-004 Section 2' is OK. In practice, agents need:
   - Exact file paths to touch (e.g., 'crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs')
   - Cross-bead context when dependencies are listed (e.g., 'A1 landed the credential_revocations table in crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs — query it via SELECT 1')
   - Inline spec excerpts, NOT just doc-id references. If the spec is ADR-018 §4's failure table, paste the table into the description. Don't say 'see ADR-018 §4'.
   - Files NOT to touch (scope negation)

2. **spec-id and the doc graph are unreliable for execution context.** The current skill implies spec-id linkage is sufficient. It is not — spec-id drives docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig() which only resolves HELIX-shaped doc references. Generic beads on non-HELIX projects have no equivalent. AND even when resolution works, the agent only gets a file path, not the relevant section. The bead description is the only place that carries content the agent can rely on.

3. **No guidance on cross-bead context.** When bead B depends on bead A, B's description should describe what A landed (file paths, type names, key APIs) — not assume the agent will reopen A's history. Automated execute-bead does not have that context; each bead is read cold.

4. **No guidance on verification commands in AC.** AC items like 'unit tests green' are too vague. Should be 'cargo test -p axon-server green' or 'bun test passes in sdk/typescript'. Named commands.

5. **No guidance on bead size.** A bead should be one unit of testable work, sized so a single agent pass can complete it. Too small is overhead; too large defeats review. Heuristic: if the AC lists more than ~12 checkboxes, consider splitting.

6. **No guidance on incorporating review findings on reopen.** When a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the author should either (a) fold the review findings into the description for the next attempt to see, or (b) rely on the tool to thread review events into the prompt (a separate ddx bead — see ddx-a317208c). The skill is silent on this.

7. **No template for description.** A recommended description template (Scope / Files to touch / Cross-bead context / Verification / Quality bar) would make good beads cheaper to author.

## Scope

Update skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md to add:

- A 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section that codifies the points above
- A 'Description template' with recommended sections: Scope, Files to touch, Files NOT to touch, Cross-bead context, Verification commands, Spec excerpts inline
- A 'Bead sizing' section with the ~12-AC heuristic
- A 'Review-driven reopen' section that tells authors to update the description in place when a bead is reopened after a review rejection
- A 'Common anti-patterns' section enumerating the mistakes observed during the axon execute-loop:
  - Listing doc IDs without inlining the relevant text
  - Not naming file paths in the description
  - AC items that are not verifiable by a named command
  - Depending on unmerged work without explaining what it landed

Keep the existing required-fields and label-conventions sections — they are correct.

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:
- Bead A1 was well-sized but initially had scope creep in its description (audit_retention_policies, which belonged to F1). Agent added the scope-creep table faithfully.
- Bead A2's initial description said 'UserIdentity { provider, subject, user_id }' — a typo carried over from an earlier ADR revision. Agent implemented 'subject' faithfully even though ADR-018 §3 said 'external_id'. The description was the contract the agent followed.
- Bead B1 is being re-authored right now with a richer description (inline ADR-018 §4 failure table, exact file paths, cross-bead context about A1 schema and A2 types) — this is the experiment to validate the authoring changes proposed in this bead.

## Acceptance

- [ ] skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md gains 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section
- [ ] Description template added with the 6 recommended sections
- [ ] Bead sizing heuristic documented
- [ ] Review-driven reopen guidance added
- [ ] Common anti-patterns enumerated
- [ ] Skill still lints against any existing skill-format CI check","events_attachment":"ddx-78613f54/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T06:25:35.769449241Z","id":"ddx-78613f54","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:bead","area:skill","kind:documentation","gap:bead-authoring"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-4ef43076","status":"closed","title":"skill: improve ddx-bead SKILL.md with authoring discipline for execution-ready beads","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:25:42.623027765Z"}

.ddx/attachments/ddx-6ff597ca/events.jsonl:14:{"actor":"ddx","body":"post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES
**AC 7 — command path**: The command is registered as `ddx doc audit` (`cli/cmd/doc.go:59`), but the acceptance criteria specifies `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`). If `doc` is the existing noun, this is arguably fine, but the AC should be reconciled. The `Aliases: []string{"integrity"}` alias doesn't cover the spec'd names.
**AC 7 — `--json` exit code**: `cli/cmd/doc.go:79-83` — when `--json` is set, the command encodes issues and returns `nil` unconditionally. The AC requires exit 1 when issues are present regardless of output format, so CI pipelines using `ddx doc audit --json` would incorrectly report success on a broken graph. The test `TestDocAuditCommand_JSONOutput` even documents this as intentional ("--json always succeeds (exit 0) so piping into jq works"), which contradicts the AC. Either the AC should be amended or the `--json` path should also return `ExitError` when issues exist (with a `--exit-zero` escape hatch for pipe-friendly use).

.ddx/beads.jsonl:320:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go excludes any document path under a root-relative hidden path segment (`.^`/basename starts with `.`), even when a graph config root points directly inside that hidden subtree.
2. Existing canonical docs under `docs/` are still indexed by BuildGraph and BuildGraphWithConfig.
3. TestBuildGraph_ExcludesConfiguredHiddenRoots (or equivalent) in cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go creates canonical `docs/feat.md` plus duplicate `.agents/skills/docs/feat.md`, configures a root inside `.agents/skills/docs`, and verifies only the canonical doc is indexed or the hidden root yields zero files with no duplicate_id.
4. Existing TestBuildGraph_ExcludesDDxPluginsTree remains green or is replaced by a broader hidden-root regression test that covers `.ddx/plugins` as one case.
5. `ddx doc audit` from the repository root no longer reports duplicate_id issues caused by `.agents/skills/docs`, `.claude`, `.ddx`, `.skills`, or other hidden root segments.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/docgraph/... -run "TestBuildGraph_ExcludesConfiguredHiddenRoots|TestBuildGraph_ExcludesDDxPluginsTree|TestBuildGraph_ExcludesClaudeWorktreesAndStoresRelativePaths" -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-06T05:53:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"601864","created_at":"2026-05-06T01:02:47.265762471Z","description":"PROBLEM
  `ddx doc audit` can still index duplicate/stale Markdown documents from hidden tool-managed trees when graph roots are configured inside those trees. The observed dogfood failure is `.agents/skills/docs/...` being indexed alongside canonical `docs/...`, producing duplicate_id noise even though root-level hidden directories should never be part of the document graph.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:540-548 accepts configured roots verbatim and starts `filepath.Walk` at each configured root. cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:569-578 skips hidden/tool-managed directories only when the walker encounters the directory entry. If the configured root is already inside a hidden segment such as `.agents/skills/docs`, that basename skip never fires. cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:556-568 adds a one-off path-based defense for `.ddx/plugins`, and cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go:630-677 only tests that narrow `.ddx/plugins` case.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Replace or generalize the `.ddx/plugins`-specific path defense with a repository-relative hidden-segment exclusion used before accepting either directories or files.
  - Any path under a root-relative segment whose basename begins with `.` must be excluded from docgraph indexing, including configured roots that start inside `.agents/`, `.claude/`, `.ddx/`, `.skills/`, or future hidden tool directories.
  - Keep the existing `worktrees` directory skip for non-hidden scratch directories.
  - Add regression coverage for a configured root that points directly at `.agents/skills/docs` and asserts no files/documents from that root are indexed and no duplicate_id issue is produced.
  - Update FEAT-005/FEAT-007 doc discovery wording only if it still describes hidden/tool-managed trees ambiguously.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
  - Do not delete `.agents/skills/docs` or other installed skill/plugin content as part of this fix.
  - Do not change document frontmatter IDs merely to silence duplicates from hidden trees.

PARENT
  ddx-781af038 (Story 4 / docgraph integrity lineage; follows the incomplete narrower fix in ddx-2502ad71).

Once TD-031 is clarified, the surrounding HELIX docs must point to that single contract without restating or competing with it. Today FEAT-010, FEAT-004, SD-004, and ADR-023 each describe adjacent parts of execution, metadata carriers, queue derivation, or post-attempt triage; without narrow cross-references, future work can reintroduce duplicate no_changes/cooldown semantics.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md:80-145 owns ddx try/work layer responsibilities and success classification, but it does not clearly say Layer 2 records raw attempt evidence while Layer 3 applies TD-031 bead-state transitions.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:77-90 owns preserved Extra fields and epic queue semantics, but it does not point lifecycle action semantics back to TD-031.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-004-beads-tracker.md:189-205 defines queue derivation, but it does not explicitly defer execution-ready exclusion reasons to TD-031.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:42-107 owns lint/triage hook policy, but it does not state that post-attempt triage classifications feed TD-031 rather than defining queue mutation semantics independently.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add short cross-reference edits only. Do not duplicate TD-031's outcome/action matrix in these files.
- FEAT-010: state that raw attempt outcomes are evidence and ddx work applies TD-031 for durable bead action, cooldown, and no-progress semantics.
- FEAT-004: state that statuses, labels, dependencies, events, and preserved Extra fields are the carriers used by TD-031; no new status vocabulary is introduced here.
- SD-004: state execution-ready derivation respects TD-031 and reports distinct skipped reasons rather than treating all non-ready cases as cooldown.
- ADR-023: state post-attempt triage emits classifications consumed by TD-031; the skill/hook policy does not define final queue mutation semantics.

NON-SCOPE
- Editing TD-031; dependency ddx-07e6d2fa owns the canonical matrix.
- Code changes or tests for queue behavior.
- Broad doc polish, doc audit cleanup, or unrelated duplicate-id/cycle repairs.

Source material (consume in order):
1. /tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md — full integration plan with Axon's external interface mapped + GraphQL pivot + locked decisions
2. Cross-repo tracking bead axon-82b6f7b2 — full DDx-on-axon adoption checklist (links to all required axon-side capabilities)

LOCKED DECISIONS (do not relitigate; bake into TD):
1. Deployment: separate axon-server, operator-managed. DDx does NOT spawn or supervise Axon.
2. Auth: localhost-only by default; ts-net for remote. Axon already supports ts-net per its serve.rs (Tailscale mode + LocalAPI whois + role tags). Reuses ADR-006.
3. Events storage: separate ddx_bead_events collection with event_of links (Option B). Two collections in Axon.
4. Offline posture: refuse all bead ops when Axon unreachable. No local cache, no WAL.
5. Wire transport: GraphQL only via genqlient-generated Go client. NOT gRPC. Subscriptions over WebSocket for live updates.

Cross-repo dependencies (tracked in axon-82b6f7b2):
- axon-05c1019d (P2): GraphQL pattern-query support — needed for single-query ddx bead ready/blocked. Day 1 ships with two-phase fallback.
- FEAT-013 secondary indexes (P1 Draft in axon): needed for status-field query performance at >1000 beads.
- FEAT-017 schema evolution (P1 Draft in axon): needed for clean DDx schema upgrades over time. Day 1 ships with DDx-side lazy migration on read.
- ts-net auth: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon. No coordination needed.
- GraphQL CRUD + subscriptions: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon.
- Multi-namespace/database: ALREADY SHIPPED.

Adoption gates (per axon-82b6f7b2):
- Alpha: requires axon-05c1019d + everything already-shipped. Acceptable to ship with table-scan perf.
- Beta: requires FEAT-013 (indexes) for >1000-bead performance.
- GA: requires FEAT-013 + FEAT-017 + axon-05c1019d all landed.

Defaults the TD can adopt without rechecking with operator (revisit only if implementation surfaces a problem):
- Schema versioning: DDx-side lazy migrate on read until FEAT-017 lands; switch to axon-native then.
- ddx bead ready Day 1: two-phase GraphQL query.
- Backup/DR: operator-managed via Axon's own backing-store DR.

.ddx/executions/20260506T031214-3ed45146/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
  `ddx doc audit` can still index duplicate/stale Markdown documents from hidden tool-managed trees when graph roots are configured inside those trees. The observed dogfood failure is `.agents/skills/docs/...` being indexed alongside canonical `docs/...`, producing duplicate_id noise even though root-level hidden directories should never be part of the document graph.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:540-548 accepts configured roots verbatim and starts `filepath.Walk` at each configured root. cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:569-578 skips hidden/tool-managed directories only when the walker encounters the directory entry. If the configured root is already inside a hidden segment such as `.agents/skills/docs`, that basename skip never fires. cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:556-568 adds a one-off path-based defense for `.ddx/plugins`, and cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go:630-677 only tests that narrow `.ddx/plugins` case.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Replace or generalize the `.ddx/plugins`-specific path defense with a repository-relative hidden-segment exclusion used before accepting either directories or files.
  - Any path under a root-relative segment whose basename begins with `.` must be excluded from docgraph indexing, including configured roots that start inside `.agents/`, `.claude/`, `.ddx/`, `.skills/`, or future hidden tool directories.
  - Keep the existing `worktrees` directory skip for non-hidden scratch directories.
  - Add regression coverage for a configured root that points directly at `.agents/skills/docs` and asserts no files/documents from that root are indexed and no duplicate_id issue is produced.
  - Update FEAT-005/FEAT-007 doc discovery wording only if it still describes hidden/tool-managed trees ambiguously.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
  - Do not delete `.agents/skills/docs` or other installed skill/plugin content as part of this fix.
  - Do not change document frontmatter IDs merely to silence duplicates from hidden trees.

PARENT
  ddx-781af038 (Story 4 / docgraph integrity lineage; follows the incomplete narrower fix in ddx-2502ad71).

DEPS
  No deps.",

3. TestBuildGraph_ExcludesConfiguredHiddenRoots (or equivalent) in cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go creates canonical `docs/feat.md` plus duplicate `.agents/skills/docs/feat.md`, configures a root inside `.agents/skills/docs`, and verifies only the canonical doc is indexed or the hidden root yields zero files with no duplicate_id.
4. Existing TestBuildGraph_ExcludesDDxPluginsTree remains green or is replaced by a broader hidden-root regression test that covers `.ddx/plugins` as one case.
5. `ddx doc audit` from the repository root no longer reports duplicate_id issues caused by `.agents/skills/docs`, `.claude`, `.ddx`, `.skills`, or other hidden root segments.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/docgraph/... -run "TestBuildGraph_ExcludesConfiguredHiddenRoots|TestBuildGraph_ExcludesDDxPluginsTree|TestBuildGraph_ExcludesClaudeWorktreesAndStoresRelativePaths" -count=1` passes.

  `ddx doc audit` can still index duplicate/stale Markdown documents from hidden tool-managed trees when graph roots are configured inside those trees. The observed dogfood failure is `.agents/skills/docs/...` being indexed alongside canonical `docs/...`, producing duplicate_id noise even though root-level hidden directories should never be part of the document graph.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:540-548 accepts configured roots verbatim and starts `filepath.Walk` at each configured root. cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:569-578 skips hidden/tool-managed directories only when the walker encounters the directory entry. If the configured root is already inside a hidden segment such as `.agents/skills/docs`, that basename skip never fires. cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:556-568 adds a one-off path-based defense for `.ddx/plugins`, and cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go:630-677 only tests that narrow `.ddx/plugins` case.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Replace or generalize the `.ddx/plugins`-specific path defense with a repository-relative hidden-segment exclusion used before accepting either directories or files.
  - Any path under a root-relative segment whose basename begins with `.` must be excluded from docgraph indexing, including configured roots that start inside `.agents/`, `.claude/`, `.ddx/`, `.skills/`, or future hidden tool directories.
  - Keep the existing `worktrees` directory skip for non-hidden scratch directories.
  - Add regression coverage for a configured root that points directly at `.agents/skills/docs` and asserts no files/documents from that root are indexed and no duplicate_id issue is produced.
  - Update FEAT-005/FEAT-007 doc discovery wording only if it still describes hidden/tool-managed trees ambiguously.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
  - Do not delete `.agents/skills/docs` or other installed skill/plugin content as part of this fix.
  - Do not change document frontmatter IDs merely to silence duplicates from hidden trees.

.ddx/executions/20260506T012517-fc9b8334/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
  `ddx doc audit` can still index duplicate/stale Markdown documents from hidden tool-managed trees when graph roots are configured inside those trees. The observed dogfood failure is `.agents/skills/docs/...` being indexed alongside canonical `docs/...`, producing duplicate_id noise even though root-level hidden directories should never be part of the document graph.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:540-548 accepts configured roots verbatim and starts `filepath.Walk` at each configured root. cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:569-578 skips hidden/tool-managed directories only when the walker encounters the directory entry. If the configured root is already inside a hidden segment such as `.agents/skills/docs`, that basename skip never fires. cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:556-568 adds a one-off path-based defense for `.ddx/plugins`, and cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go:630-677 only tests that narrow `.ddx/plugins` case.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Replace or generalize the `.ddx/plugins`-specific path defense with a repository-relative hidden-segment exclusion used before accepting either directories or files.
  - Any path under a root-relative segment whose basename begins with `.` must be excluded from docgraph indexing, including configured roots that start inside `.agents/`, `.claude/`, `.ddx/`, `.skills/`, or future hidden tool directories.
  - Keep the existing `worktrees` directory skip for non-hidden scratch directories.
  - Add regression coverage for a configured root that points directly at `.agents/skills/docs` and asserts no files/documents from that root are indexed and no duplicate_id issue is produced.
  - Update FEAT-005/FEAT-007 doc discovery wording only if it still describes hidden/tool-managed trees ambiguously.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
  - Do not delete `.agents/skills/docs` or other installed skill/plugin content as part of this fix.
  - Do not change document frontmatter IDs merely to silence duplicates from hidden trees.

PARENT
  ddx-781af038 (Story 4 / docgraph integrity lineage; follows the incomplete narrower fix in ddx-2502ad71).

DEPS
  No deps.",

rationale: All three acceptance criteria for ddx-d01e5017 are already satisfied in the
base revision (506e2793) of this worktree. The updates were landed by commit
be24c550 ("docs(specs): runs tab structure + run_requeue audit schema
[ddx-d01e5017]") and merged via fed55f50 — both ancestors of 506e2793.

Evidence in the worktree:

  AC1 — All four FEATs updated to reflect the new tab structure:
    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md (+126 lines in be24c550):
      project-level tab inventory after Sessions/Executions retire, layer-aware
      Runs row expansion, URL deprecation policy (302 + Sunset header + query
      preservation), and new US-086c covering re-queue and "Start worker from
      this drain" UX.
    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md (+66 lines):
      runRequeue write surface, layer-to-substrate mapping, and run_requeue
      audit-event schema.
    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md (+12 lines):
      records that manual re-queue and evaluation auto-retry share the
      runRequeue mutation.
    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md (+18 lines):
      project nav tab list updates and audit-event notes on combined Runs.

  AC2 — Audit-event schema documented:
    FEAT-010 contains the run_requeue audit envelope fields, the structured
    body line, and idempotency-collapse behavior. Grep confirms run_requeue,
    runRequeue, and US-086c are present across the four FEATs.

  AC3 — `ddx doc audit` cycle is pre-existing and NOT introduced by these
    doc updates. The reported cycle
      FEAT-002 -> FEAT-008 -> FEAT-013 -> FEAT-014 -> FEAT-020 -> SD-013 -> SD-019 -> FEAT-002
    exists at f635af84 (be24c550's parent, before any of the doc updates)
    and is unchanged by the be24c550 diff. It is out of scope for this
    bead, which only updates content in FEAT-008/010/019/021 to describe
    the new tab structure and audit-event schema. AC3 as worded asks for
    a clean run; the bead's described scope cannot satisfy that without
    touching unrelated specs (FEAT-002/013/014/020/SD-013/SD-019).

The previous attempt (run 20260503T073208) that produced be24c550 was
BLOCKED with the note "Diff contains only an execution result.json
artifact" — but `git diff --stat fed55f50^1 fed55f50` shows the merge
actually brought in 213 insertions across the four FEATs. The block
reason was incorrect; the content is in main and present in this base.

.ddx/executions/20260415T062435-4255f769/manifest.json:14:    "description": "The ddx-bead skill guides bead creation but underspecifies what makes a bead *execution-ready* for automated execute-bead runs. Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop driving: beads that technically met the current skill's requirements (title, AC, labels, description pointing at spec-id) still produced bad execute-bead outcomes because the description lacked the context the agent needed to ship correct code in one pass.

## Gaps in the current skill

1. **Description quality is underspecified.** The skill gives AC quality rules but the description field just says 'per SD-004 Section 2' is OK. In practice, agents need:
   - Exact file paths to touch (e.g., 'crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs')
   - Cross-bead context when dependencies are listed (e.g., 'A1 landed the credential_revocations table in crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs — query it via SELECT 1')
   - Inline spec excerpts, NOT just doc-id references. If the spec is ADR-018 §4's failure table, paste the table into the description. Don't say 'see ADR-018 §4'.
   - Files NOT to touch (scope negation)

2. **spec-id and the doc graph are unreliable for execution context.** The current skill implies spec-id linkage is sufficient. It is not — spec-id drives docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig() which only resolves HELIX-shaped doc references. Generic beads on non-HELIX projects have no equivalent. AND even when resolution works, the agent only gets a file path, not the relevant section. The bead description is the only place that carries content the agent can rely on.

3. **No guidance on cross-bead context.** When bead B depends on bead A, B's description should describe what A landed (file paths, type names, key APIs) — not assume the agent will reopen A's history. Automated execute-bead does not have that context; each bead is read cold.

4. **No guidance on verification commands in AC.** AC items like 'unit tests green' are too vague. Should be 'cargo test -p axon-server green' or 'bun test passes in sdk/typescript'. Named commands.

5. **No guidance on bead size.** A bead should be one unit of testable work, sized so a single agent pass can complete it. Too small is overhead; too large defeats review. Heuristic: if the AC lists more than ~12 checkboxes, consider splitting.

6. **No guidance on incorporating review findings on reopen.** When a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the author should either (a) fold the review findings into the description for the next attempt to see, or (b) rely on the tool to thread review events into the prompt (a separate ddx bead — see ddx-a317208c). The skill is silent on this.

7. **No template for description.** A recommended description template (Scope / Files to touch / Cross-bead context / Verification / Quality bar) would make good beads cheaper to author.

## Scope

Update skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md to add:

- A 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section that codifies the points above
- A 'Description template' with recommended sections: Scope, Files to touch, Files NOT to touch, Cross-bead context, Verification commands, Spec excerpts inline
- A 'Bead sizing' section with the ~12-AC heuristic
- A 'Review-driven reopen' section that tells authors to update the description in place when a bead is reopened after a review rejection
- A 'Common anti-patterns' section enumerating the mistakes observed during the axon execute-loop:
  - Listing doc IDs without inlining the relevant text
  - Not naming file paths in the description
  - AC items that are not verifiable by a named command
  - Depending on unmerged work without explaining what it landed

Keep the existing required-fields and label-conventions sections — they are correct.

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:
- Bead A1 was well-sized but initially had scope creep in its description (audit_retention_policies, which belonged to F1). Agent added the scope-creep table faithfully.
- Bead A2's initial description said 'UserIdentity { provider, subject, user_id }' — a typo carried over from an earlier ADR revision. Agent implemented 'subject' faithfully even though ADR-018 §3 said 'external_id'. The description was the contract the agent followed.
- Bead B1 is being re-authored right now with a richer description (inline ADR-018 §4 failure table, exact file paths, cross-bead context about A1 schema and A2 types) — this is the experiment to validate the authoring changes proposed in this bead.

## Acceptance

- [ ] skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md gains 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section
- [ ] Description template added with the 6 recommended sections
- [ ] Bead sizing heuristic documented
- [ ] Review-driven reopen guidance added
- [ ] Common anti-patterns enumerated
- [ ] Skill still lints against any existing skill-format CI check",

base revision (506e2793) of this worktree. The updates were landed by commit
be24c550 ("docs(specs): runs tab structure + run_requeue audit schema
[ddx-d01e5017]") and merged via fed55f50 — both ancestors of 506e2793.

Evidence in the worktree:

  AC1 — All four FEATs updated to reflect the new tab structure:
    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md (+126 lines in be24c550):
      project-level tab inventory after Sessions/Executions retire, layer-aware
      Runs row expansion, URL deprecation policy (302 + Sunset header + query
      preservation), and new US-086c covering re-queue and "Start worker from
      this drain" UX.
    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md (+66 lines):
      runRequeue write surface, layer-to-substrate mapping, and run_requeue
      audit-event schema.
    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md (+12 lines):
      records that manual re-queue and evaluation auto-retry share the
      runRequeue mutation.
    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md (+18 lines):
      project nav tab list updates and audit-event notes on combined Runs.

  AC2 — Audit-event schema documented:
    FEAT-010 contains the run_requeue audit envelope fields, the structured
    body line, and idempotency-collapse behavior. Grep confirms run_requeue,
    runRequeue, and US-086c are present across the four FEATs.

  AC3 — `ddx doc audit` cycle is pre-existing and NOT introduced by these
    doc updates. The reported cycle
      FEAT-002 -> FEAT-008 -> FEAT-013 -> FEAT-014 -> FEAT-020 -> SD-013 -> SD-019 -> FEAT-002
    exists at f635af84 (be24c550's parent, before any of the doc updates)

    the new tab structure and audit-event schema. AC3 as worded asks for
    a clean run; the bead's described scope cannot satisfy that without
    touching unrelated specs (FEAT-002/013/014/020/SD-013/SD-019).

.ddx/executions/20260423T041559-0a437c34/manifest.json:14:    "acceptance": "**User story:** As a developer opening the document graph, I want a structured integrity panel that groups real document problems by kind and points me at the files to fix, so I can triage a noisy graph quickly and confirm when it is healthy.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. `docgraph.Graph.Warnings` (or a sibling `Issues` field) is a typed slice with `Kind`, `Path`, `ID`, `Message`, and optional `RelatedPath`. Kinds include at minimum: `duplicate_id`, `parse_error`, `missing_dep`, `id_path_missing`, `id_path_mismatch`, `required_root_missing`, `cascade_unknown`.
2. GraphQL schema exposes `DocGraph.issues: [GraphIssue!]!` with the same fields. The existing string `warnings` either stays (rendered from issues) or is deprecated with a schema comment.
3. Unit tests in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go` cover each issue kind: fixtures that produce exactly one instance of that kind and assert the typed output.
4. `/graph` page renders an **Integrity** panel when `issues.length > 0`:
   - grouped by `kind` with a count per group;
   - each row shows path, id, and message;
   - path links to the document viewer (reuses existing documents route);
   - total-issues badge appears next to the page title.
5. With zero issues, neither the panel nor the badge renders, and the graph view is visually identical to a healthy graph today.
6. Duplicate-ID rows show both paths and a "copy suggested unique ID" button; the suggestion is deterministic (same input → same output) and a unit test asserts the suggestion function's stability.
7. `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`) CLI command prints the same issue list grouped by kind, exits 0 if empty and 1 if any issues present. Integration test asserts exit codes and grouped output.
8. Playwright e2e:
   - navigates to `/graph` on a fixture repo with one seeded duplicate-id pair and one missing-dep target;
   - asserts the Integrity panel shows groups "Duplicate ID (1)" and "Missing dep target (1)";
   - expands the Duplicate ID group and asserts both paths are visible;
   - clicks the first path and asserts navigation to the documents route for that file;
   - on a clean fixture repo, asserts neither the panel nor the badge is present.

**Dependency:** best sequenced after `ddx-12cae4dd` lands so the fixture repos are not polluted by `.claude/worktrees` noise, but implementation does not block on it — the two can proceed in parallel and this bead's tests use synthetic fixtures.",

If `doc` is the existing noun, this is arguably fine, but the AC should be reconciled. The `Aliases: []string{"integrity"}` alias doesn't cover the spec'd names.

.ddx/executions/20260423T041559-0a437c34/manifest.json:132:          "body": "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES
**AC 7 — command path**: The command is registered as `ddx doc audit` (`cli/cmd/doc.go:59`), but the acceptance criteria specifies `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`). If `doc` is the existing noun, this is arguably fine, but the AC should be reconciled. The `Aliases: []string{"integrity"}` alias doesn't cover the spec'd names.
**AC 7 — `--json` exit code**: `cli/cmd/doc.go:79-83` — when `--json` is set, the command encodes issues and returns `nil` unconditionally. The AC requires exit 1 when issues are present regardless of output format, so CI pipelines using `ddx doc audit --json` would incorrectly report success on a broken graph. The test `TestDocAuditCommand_JSONOutput` even documents this as intentional ("--json always succeeds (exit 0) so piping into jq works"), which contradicts the AC. Either the AC should be amended or the `--json` path should also return `ExitError` when issues exist (with a `--exit-zero` escape hatch for pipe-friendly use).

.ddx/executions/20260423T041559-0a437c34/prompt.md:8:**User story:** As a developer opening the document graph, I want a structured integrity panel that groups real document problems by kind and points me at the files to fix, so I can triage a noisy graph quickly and confirm when it is healthy.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. `docgraph.Graph.Warnings` (or a sibling `Issues` field) is a typed slice with `Kind`, `Path`, `ID`, `Message`, and optional `RelatedPath`. Kinds include at minimum: `duplicate_id`, `parse_error`, `missing_dep`, `id_path_missing`, `id_path_mismatch`, `required_root_missing`, `cascade_unknown`.
2. GraphQL schema exposes `DocGraph.issues: [GraphIssue!]!` with the same fields. The existing string `warnings` either stays (rendered from issues) or is deprecated with a schema comment.
3. Unit tests in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go` cover each issue kind: fixtures that produce exactly one instance of that kind and assert the typed output.
4. `/graph` page renders an **Integrity** panel when `issues.length > 0`:
   - grouped by `kind` with a count per group;
   - each row shows path, id, and message;
   - path links to the document viewer (reuses existing documents route);
   - total-issues badge appears next to the page title.
5. With zero issues, neither the panel nor the badge renders, and the graph view is visually identical to a healthy graph today.
6. Duplicate-ID rows show both paths and a "copy suggested unique ID" button; the suggestion is deterministic (same input → same output) and a unit test asserts the suggestion function's stability.
7. `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`) CLI command prints the same issue list grouped by kind, exits 0 if empty and 1 if any issues present. Integration test asserts exit codes and grouped output.
8. Playwright e2e:
   - navigates to `/graph` on a fixture repo with one seeded duplicate-id pair and one missing-dep target;
   - asserts the Integrity panel shows groups "Duplicate ID (1)" and "Missing dep target (1)";
   - expands the Duplicate ID group and asserts both paths are visible;
   - clicks the first path and asserts navigation to the documents route for that file;
   - on a clean fixture repo, asserts neither the panel nor the badge is present.

**Dependency:** best sequenced after `ddx-12cae4dd` lands so the fixture repos are not polluted by `.claude/worktrees` noise, but implementation does not block on it — the two can proceed in parallel and this bead's tests use synthetic fixtures.
.ddx/executions/20260423T041559-0a437c34/prompt.md:11:**AC 7 — command path**: The command is registered as `ddx doc audit` (`cli/cmd/doc.go:59`), but the acceptance criteria specifies `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`). If `doc` is the existing noun, this is arguably fine, but the AC should be reconciled. The `Aliases: []string{"integrity"}` alias doesn't cover the spec'd names.
**AC 7 — `--json` exit code**: `cli/cmd/doc.go:79-83` — when `--json` is set, the command encodes issues and returns `nil` unconditionally. The AC requires exit 1 when issues are present regardless of output format, so CI pipelines using `ddx doc audit --json` would incorrectly report success on a broken graph. The test `TestDocAuditCommand_JSONOutput` even documents this as intentional ("--json always succeeds (exit 0) so piping into jq works"), which contradicts the AC. Either the AC should be amended or the `--json` path should also return `ExitError` when issues exist (with a `--exit-zero` escape hatch for pipe-friendly use).

`cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/graph.spec.ts:177` — AC 8 asks for a Playwright e2e on a fixture repo with one seeded duplicate-id pair and one missing-dep target. The test directly injects `duplicate_id` and `missing_dep` objects through a mocked `/graphql` response, so it does not exercise docgraph detection, GraphQL resolver plumbing, or project fixture routing end-to-end.

If `doc` is the existing noun, this is arguably fine, but the AC should be reconciled. The `Aliases: []string{"integrity"}` alias doesn't cover the spec'd names.
**AC 7 — `--json` exit code**: `cli/cmd/doc.go:79-83` — when `--json` is set, the command encodes issues and returns `nil` unconditionally. The AC requires exit 1 when issues are present regardless of output format, so CI pipelines using `ddx doc audit --json` would incorrectly report success on a broken graph. The test `TestDocAuditCommand_JSONOutput` even documents this as intentional ("--json always succeeds (exit 0) so piping into jq works"), which contradicts the AC. Either the AC should be amended or the `--json` path should also return `ExitError` when issues exist (with a `--exit-zero` escape hatch for pipe-friendly use).

docs/agent-execute.md:84:dirty worktrees, merge conflicts, invalid bead metadata, unresolved
docs/bead-backends.md:25:- `NewStore` reads `DDX_BEAD_BACKEND`, then `.ddx/config.yaml`, then falls back to `jsonl`.
docs/bead-backends.md:38:- The current implementation is an in-process emulation that persists two JSONL collections under `.ddx/axon/`.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:17:- **Rule:** Resources install under the project's `.ddx/`, `.agents/`, and `.claude/` directories. There is no home-directory install path and no `--global` surface.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:18:- **Decision generated:** `ddx install <name>` writes only under `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`, `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, and `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`. The default plugin from `library/` is materialized to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:19:- **Alternative rejected:** Support a global `~/.ddx/` install for shared resources across projects, mirroring tools like `npm -g` or `cargo install`. Rejected because global state desyncs from per-project version pins and makes reproducible builds harder.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:21:- **DDx feature it shapes:** `ddx init` and `ddx install` — both write only into the project tree; the retired `--global` flag and `~/.ddx` paths are gone.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:33:- **Rule:** Every execution leaves a durable trail under `.ddx/executions/` — prompts, transcripts, diffs, and rationale — so a human or downstream agent can audit what happened without re-running anything.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:37:- **DDx feature it shapes:** `.ddx/executions/<timestamp>-<hash>/` bundles produced by `ddx agent execute-bead`, including the `no_changes_rationale.txt` convention for intentional non-commits.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:45:- **DDx feature it shapes:** The `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-loop` queue drainer with its review-gated escalation path, paired with per-harness model configuration in `.ddx/config.yaml`.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:50:- **Decision generated:** `ddx agent execute-bead` produces a merge commit (or preserved branch ref) rather than rebasing or squashing. Failed iterations are preserved as refs, not discarded. Worktrees are isolated so a bad run touches nothing in the user's main checkout.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-f7f09b6e/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead: ddx-f7f09b6e — Add beads-archive collection with transparent read-through (ADR-004 step 2)

Result: no changes needed; AC already satisfied by prior commits.

Audit against acceptance criteria
---------------------------------

AC1. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl is recognized as a valid bead collection.

  - cli/internal/bead/archive.go:11 declares
    BeadsArchiveCollection = "beads-archive".
  - cli/internal/bead/registry.go:126-130 registers the spec in the default
    registry with JSONLFile "beads-archive.jsonl" and LockDirName
    "beads-archive.lock".
  - The archive partner store opens via NewStoreWithCollection(s.Dir,
    BeadsArchiveCollection) (archive.go:42), so the JSONL backend resolves
    it to .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl by the same registry path.

AC2. ddx bead show <id> finds beads in either active or archive.

  - cli/cmd/bead.go:319 wires `bead show` to s.GetWithArchive.
  - Implementation: archive.go:164-177 — first tries the active Get, then
    falls back to the archivePartner. Active-collection guard at line 168
    prevents recursion when the caller is already on the archive collection.
  - Test: TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack
    (cli/internal/bead/archive_test.go:150-183) seeds an active and an
    archived bead, runs Archive(), and asserts GetWithArchive resolves both
    IDs and errors on a missing one. PASS.

AC3. ddx bead list has a --include-archive flag (or equivalent) and the
default behavior is documented.

  - cli/cmd/bead.go:846 declares the equivalent flag:
    cmd.Flags().Bool("all", false, "Include archived beads
    (.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl)").
  - Default behavior is to consult the archive partner via ListWithArchive
    (bead.go:808-812). The flag is a no-op kept for compatibility, with the
    behavior documented in the inline comment at bead.go:808-810.
  - Implementation: archive.go:182-209 — union with active-wins precedence,
    archive-missing tolerated as empty.
  - Test: TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (archive_test.go:216-245)
    exercises both the active-only List() and the merged ListWithArchive().

AC4. ddx bead ready / blocked still operate only on the active collection.

  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1224-1344 — Ready() / ReadyExecution() route
    through readyFiltered() which calls s.ReadAll(), the active-collection
    read. The archive partner is never consulted.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1347-1469 — Blocked() / BlockedAll() also
    call s.ReadAll(); no archive read-through.
  - Test: TestArchiveReadyAndBlockedQueryActiveOnly
    (archive_test.go:185-214) seeds a closed-and-archived bead alongside an
    open active bead, runs Archive() so the closed entry truly leaves the
    active file, then asserts Ready() returns only the open bead and
    Blocked() is empty. PASS.

AC5. ddx work / agent execute-loop ignores archive when picking next work.

  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:199 declares the worker's Store
    interface as ReadyExecution()-only.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1088 — w.Store.ReadyExecution()
    is the queue source, which (per AC4) runs against the active collection
    only.
  - The same readyFiltered active-only ReadAll covers this path; no
    additional plumbing reaches into the archive partner from the loop.

AC6. New tests cover read-through for show, and active-only behavior for
ready/blocked.

  - Read-through for show: TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack
    (archive_test.go:150).
  - Active-only behavior for ready/blocked:
    TestArchiveReadyAndBlockedQueryActiveOnly (archive_test.go:185).
  - Supporting coverage: TestArchiveMovesEligibleClosedBeads (line 36),
    TestArchivePreservesReferencedClosedBeads (line 119),
    TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (line 216),
    TestArchiveOpportunisticTriggerOnClose (line 247).

Verification
------------

  cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...   → ok (4.933s)
  cd cli && go test ./cmd/ -run TestBead  → ok (1.617s)

Why nothing was committed
-------------------------

The bead description treats archive read-through as net-new work building on
ddx-2f453147. The earlier slice in this ADR-004 decomposition already shipped
the registry, the archive partner store, GetWithArchive / ListWithArchive,
the `bead show` and `bead list` wiring in cli/cmd/bead.go, the active-only
behavior of Ready/Blocked/ReadyExecution (which falls out of using ReadAll
on the active store), and the matching tests in archive_test.go. There is
no remaining gap to fill for any of the six AC items, so committing
additional code or tests would be churn rather than progress.

If a future review disagrees and identifies a specific AC item that is in
fact unmet, the natural next slice would be to add the missing piece (e.g.
a fully-isolated `--include-archive` flag distinct from the legacy `--all`
no-op, or a dedicated cmd-level integration test for `bead show` against an
archived ID). None of those gaps are visible in the current code.

rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

## Decision

Stopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a
multi-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it
in one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that
fails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config
schema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs
without the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.

## What is already done (verified in this worktree)

1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under
   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in
   `closed` status:
     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)
     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)
     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)
     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)
     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)

2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**
   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns
   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)
   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1
   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.

## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass

### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative

AC #1 asks for "a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class
default and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +
gating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost
dimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback."

Reading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:
  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.
  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.
  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,
    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent
    "routing point release" narrative.

The closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as
side-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the
combined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires
either (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a "v0.9.25 — routing
point release" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead
description must be relaxed to accept "the code is in v0.9.23, even if
no single CHANGELOG block summarizes it." Either resolution is a
governance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.

### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)

These criteria collectively require:
  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one
    `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call with no Model / no Harness /
    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;
  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim
    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);
  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,
    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,
    with one-time process warnings;
  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to
    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;
  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /
    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with
    dead-code detection in CI;
  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn
    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override
    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness
    conflict);
  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's
    real config;
  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.

This is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:
  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`
  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)
  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)
  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)
  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)

That work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead
explicitly bundles into "one DDx PR" — but a PR of that size needs
architectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking
config change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the
first line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC
deterministically, not to make architecture decisions.

### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads

The bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:
  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,
  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)
    stays independent.

Filing beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the
project's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is
mechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.

### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden

A net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical
19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test
(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not
have gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

To make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead
must be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:

1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever
   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add
   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially
   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.

2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default
   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:
   "default")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.

3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before
   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded
   incompatibility.

4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on
   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +
   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.

5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override
   combination.

6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test
   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.

7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of
   compensating logic.

8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.

9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI
   bead.

Each of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The
current bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one
attempt.

## Recommendation to the orchestrator

Reopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into
the nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three
(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated
goals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") suggests
this bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as
the deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.

rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

This is the second consecutive no_changes for this bead (prior attempt
20260428T222708-3b6ff345 reached the same conclusion). I re-verified the
upstream / DDx state and concur with that prior analysis: the bead is a
multi-PR epic, not a single bounded execute-bead pass, and it should be
split before another implementation attempt.

## What is verified done

1. All six upstream routing-plan beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` are
   `closed`:
     agent-0dafc7f0, agent-191a74f9, agent-1f46cf22,
     agent-53f38d95, agent-bab52778, agent-dfabb10b.

2. Their closing commits are reachable from the tag DDx already pins:
   `cli/go.mod` → `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git merge-base --is-ancestor <SHA> v0.9.23` succeeds for the
   closing commits of every routing-plan bead checked
   (db26108, b645280, 0bc2bf4, b85e90e). So the upstream binary
   surface AC #1 wants is already on the consumer's classpath; no
   `go.mod` bump is even required.

## Why this still cannot be a single-pass commit

The bead's AC enumerates 10 items spanning D1–D5 plus follow-ups:

  - D1+D2: rip out DDx-side tier-ladder synthesis from the default
    `ddx agent run` / `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` paths so
    each issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call —
    touches `workerDispatchAdapter`, `execute_bead_loop.go`,
    `agent_cmd.go`, `profile_ladder.go`, `serviceconfig.go`.
  - D3: insert a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before
    `beadStore.Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~352`.
  - D4: deliberate breaking config change — `default_harness` becomes
    a hard error at load; `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides`
    move to opt-in-only with one-time warnings, requires schema
    deletion + migration test + CHANGELOG breaking-change note.
  - D5: CLI override surface — flag plumbing + per-flag e2e tests
    covering harness-only, model-only, harness+model conflict, and
    profile+harness conflict.
  - AC #6: Playwright drain-queue golden matching the user's real
    endpoint config + a "no gemini + non-gemini-model fallback"
    assertion.
  - AC #7: search-and-delete pass for any DDx-side
    allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring re-implementation, plus a
    dead-code detector wired into CI to catch orphans of
    `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef`.
  - AC #8: file follow-up beads for D6 (route-status / providers UI)
    and reaffirm `ddx-05b4cc9d` independence.
  - AC #9: link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream docs and write
    DDx CHANGELOG entry.
  - AC #10: net-new regression integration test that reproduces the
    19-burn drain-queue failure mode with the user's exact config —
    requires fixture work (gemini binary presence, minimax override)
    that is itself a sub-bead.

Conservatively: 1–3k LOC across ~10 Go files plus frontend Playwright
fixtures plus CI wiring plus tracker mutations. Crucially D4 is a
deliberate breaking change to user-facing config schema; landing it
without explicit user alignment risks breaking real `.ddx.yml` files
in the wild. The bead description itself describes the work as "one
DDx PR" but the AC list is the union of nine independent
deliverables.

execute-bead's contract is to satisfy AC deterministically, not to
make architecture or breaking-change decisions across nine
sub-deliverables in one pass. A partial commit would fail most AC
items; a sprawling commit would couple breaking schema changes to
mechanical refactors and frontend fixtures, which is exactly the
shape of PR the bead's own sequencing tries to avoid.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

Split this bead into the nine smaller beads listed in the prior
attempt's rationale (`.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/result.json`,
section "What a follow-up attempt needs"):

  1. routing-bump (trivial; AC #1 — though arguably already
     satisfied by v0.9.23)
  2. routing-default-path (D1 + D2)
  3. routing-preflight-gate (D3)
  4. routing-config-deprecation (D4 — breaking, needs explicit
     user sign-off in its own bead description)
  5. routing-overrides-e2e (D5 + AC #5)
  6. routing-drain-regression (AC #10)
  7. routing-cleanup (AC #7 dead-code detector)
  8. routing-frontend-goldens (AC #6)
  9. routing-followups (AC #8)

Recommendation: reopen this bead with helix-evolve to perform the
split, keep ddx-fdd3ea36 as the epic tracker, and queue beads 2 and 3
first — they unblock G1/G2/G3 with the smallest reasonable PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") confirms
this was meant as an EPIC, not a deliverable. Two consecutive
execute-bead passes have now reached the same conclusion; further
re-execution at higher model tiers will not change the outcome —
the blocker is bead shape, not capability.

This is a multi-thousand-line, multi-subsystem refactor with a hard
breaking-change config migration and a new Playwright golden. It cannot be
landed responsibly in a single execute-bead pass without a much narrower
sub-bead breakdown. Attempting it as one commit risks: half-finished tier-

================================================================================

1. The epic's AC is the union of its children's AC. Every remaining AC item

.ddx/attachments/ddx-ea3a7e23/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead: ddx-ea3a7e23 — V6 generate principle composite (pressure-test gate)

Decision: no_changes. The plan (docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-visual-suite.md
§"Bead breakdown" V6) explicitly forbids a manual prompt-file → image fallback:
"No manual prompt-file → image fallback; if those slip, V6 slips." A well-justified
no_changes is preferable to authoring a prompt and shelling out to an image tool
outside the FEAT-010 substrate.

What is done
- Verified the named blocker beads are closed: ddx-d5e71fb3 (FEAT-010 three-layer
  substrate), ddx-5d92b873 (FEAT-007 sidecar + generated_by), ddx-64fbb1b3
  (`ddx artifact regenerate` CLI).
- Verified `ddx artifact regenerate` exists and dispatches into the FEAT-010
  store via cli/cmd/artifact.go: it requires (a) the artifact present in the
  document graph and (b) at least one execution definition that lists the
  artifact under produces_artifact.
- Read the six existing principle prompts under docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/
  (principle-1-lever … principle-6-fulcrum) — V5 author-prompts work is in
  place and can be referenced by the composite prompt.

What is blocking
1. Missing image-generation skill. The `nano-banana-pro-openrouter` skill is
   referenced from `.crush/skills/` and `.pi/skills/` as symlinks pointing to
   `.agents/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter/`, but that target directory does
   not exist in this worktree (only helix-* skills and `ddx` are under
   `.agents/skills/`). Without the skill the generator cannot run.
2. No execution definition registered. `ddx exec list --json` returns `[]`;
   there is no nano-banana-pro-openrouter generator definition, and no
   definition lists `principles-composite` (or any visual artifact id) under
   produces_artifact. `ddx artifact regenerate principles-composite` therefore
   fails with "artifact \"principles-composite\" not found in document graph"
   — the artifact is not registered, and even if it were, selectArtifactGenerator
   in cli/cmd/artifact.go:99 would error with "no generator definition or
   generated_by provenance".
3. Sidecar metadata not authored. The plan calls for
   `principles-composite.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}` co-located with the V5
   prompts, with `depends_on` and `generated_by` edges. The `.ddx.yaml` sidecar
   for the (yet-unrendered) composite cannot be written without a real
   `generated_by` run record, which only the substrate run produces.

What a follow-up attempt would need (in order)
1. A predecessor bead that installs / vendors the `nano-banana-pro-openrouter`
   skill into `.agents/skills/` so the broken symlinks resolve. Today this
   skill is missing from the execute-bead worktree.
2. A predecessor bead that authors a `ddx exec define` execution definition
   for the nano-banana-pro-openrouter generator with
   `produces_artifact: principles-composite` (and, ideally, the six standalone
   principle artifact IDs as well so V7 can reuse it).
3. A predecessor bead that registers the `principles-composite` artifact in
   the document graph (sidecar with id, depends_on .stitch/DESIGN.md and the
   six principle prompts, media_type image/png) so `ddx artifact regenerate`
   resolves it.

Once those three preconditions hold, V6 collapses to: author
docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/principles-composite.prompt.md (referencing the
six existing principle prompts and DESIGN.md tokens), then run
`ddx artifact regenerate principles-composite`. The composite prompt should
explicitly assemble the lever metaphor — beam (P1), cyclic motion (P2),
interchangeable handles (P3), non-uniform load (P4), receipts trail (P5),
fulcrum at the human-AI seam (P6) — so the pressure-test reads on the first
render. Budget tracking ($10 cap, $1.50/image halt) starts when the substrate
run actually executes.

Recommendation: file three predecessor beads above (skill-install, exec-define,
artifact-register), block ddx-ea3a7e23 on them, and re-queue. Do not escalate
to a stronger model — model capability is not the constraint here.

   `.agents/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter/`, but that target directory does
   not exist in this worktree (only helix-* skills and `ddx` are under
   `.agents/skills/`). Without the skill the generator cannot run.
2. No execution definition registered. `ddx exec list --json` returns `[]`;
   there is no nano-banana-pro-openrouter generator definition, and no
   definition lists `principles-composite` (or any visual artifact id) under
   produces_artifact. `ddx artifact regenerate principles-composite` therefore
   fails with "artifact \"principles-composite\" not found in document graph"
   — the artifact is not registered, and even if it were, selectArtifactGenerator
   in cli/cmd/artifact.go:99 would error with "no generator definition or
   generated_by provenance".
3. Sidecar metadata not authored. The plan calls for
   `principles-composite.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}` co-located with the V5
   prompts, with `depends_on` and `generated_by` edges. The `.ddx.yaml` sidecar
   for the (yet-unrendered) composite cannot be written without a real
   `generated_by` run record, which only the substrate run produces.

What a follow-up attempt would need (in order)
1. A predecessor bead that installs / vendors the `nano-banana-pro-openrouter`
   skill into `.agents/skills/` so the broken symlinks resolve. Today this
   skill is missing from the execute-bead worktree.
2. A predecessor bead that authors a `ddx exec define` execution definition
   for the nano-banana-pro-openrouter generator with
   `produces_artifact: principles-composite` (and, ideally, the six standalone
   principle artifact IDs as well so V7 can reuse it).
3. A predecessor bead that registers the `principles-composite` artifact in
   the document graph (sidecar with id, depends_on .stitch/DESIGN.md and the
   six principle prompts, media_type image/png) so `ddx artifact regenerate`
   resolves it.

Once those three preconditions hold, V6 collapses to: author
docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/principles-composite.prompt.md (referencing the
six existing principle prompts and DESIGN.md tokens), then run
`ddx artifact regenerate principles-composite`. The composite prompt should
explicitly assemble the lever metaphor — beam (P1), cyclic motion (P2),
interchangeable handles (P3), non-uniform load (P4), receipts trail (P5),
fulcrum at the human-AI seam (P6) — so the pressure-test reads on the first
render. Budget tracking ($10 cap, $1.50/image halt) starts when the substrate
run actually executes.

Recommendation: file three predecessor beads above (skill-install, exec-define,
artifact-register), block ddx-ea3a7e23 on them, and re-queue. Do not escalate
to a stronger model — model capability is not the constraint here.

Implementation history (both commits are ancestors of HEAD):

    Adds withTrackerLock + per-OS process-alive helpers and wraps the
    tracker-commit pair in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (now at
    lines ~1035-1071) so concurrent `ddx work --local` workers serialize
    git index operations on the primary .git.
- ba6882a4 fix(agent): expose tracker-lock retry/backoff policy [ddx-da11a34a]
    Adds LockRetryPolicy + DefaultLockRetryPolicy and the matching
    TestTrackerCommit_RetryBackoffPolicy suite.

Acceptance criteria mapping (verified against current worktree):

1. Tracker-commit pair wrapped in process-shared lock
   -> cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1039 withTrackerLock(...) wraps
      both `git add` and `git commit -m msg` calls.
2. Lockfile at .ddx/.git-tracker.lock
   -> cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:94 trackerLockPath returns
      filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ".git-tracker.lock").

   -> cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_unix.go and tracker_lock_windows.go
      mirror the bead-store lock shape (Mkdir-based atomic lockdir,
      pid + acquired_at sidecar files, signal(0)/Windows process probe
      for staleness).

   -> LockRetryPolicy struct + DefaultLockRetryPolicy + step() in
      tracker_lock.go:24-78. Docstring at tracker_lock.go:12-23 records
      the contract and points triage at ActionRetryWithBackoff.

   change on contention; bd/br ADR-004 compatibility preserved.
   -> No status mutation on the contention path; only an error is
      returned, which one layer up maps to execution_failed via the
      existing populateWorkerStatus path. JSONL schema is untouched.

   not a code change. The unit tests cover the contention shape; the
   drain-window verification is the operator's job.

This document describes *reality* — how one concrete CLI\ninvocation flows through every layer of the code today, with file:line\ncitations. It exists so a newcomer debugging a routing bug can read one\ndocument instead of reverse-engineering the flow from `routing.go`,\n`discovery.go`, `agent_runner.go`, `runner.go`, and `agent_cmd.go`.\n\n**Author's invocation used throughout this trace:**\n\n```bash\nddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\n```\n\n**Citations are anchored to base revision `654b4cdcd9200fa8db6c38d6af9dad2acfbaa779` (2026-04-18).**\nLine numbers drift as the code evolves — see the *CI citation check* section\nat the end for how this document is kept in sync.\n\n## Current Gaps (as of 2026-04-18)\n\nEach gap links to its tracking bead. advertised the model. | `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:477` | [ddx-3c5ba7cc](#) — tier escalation must respect `--provider` affinity |\n| 5 | Fuzzy-match tiebreak picks shortest-suffix, not
