RunAgent-->>User: Result (stdout + session log)\n```\n\n## Layer 1 — CLI flag parse\n\n`ddx work` is a thin alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`. All flags are cloned\nfrom the inner command.\n\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:10` — `newWorkCommand` constructs the alias.\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:40` — flags are cloned verbatim from `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand`.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1319` — `--harness` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1320` — `--model` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1321` — `--provider` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1322` — `--model-ref` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1327` — `--local` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1338` — `runAgentExecuteLoop` entry point reads flags.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1342` through `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1357` — flag values\n  are plucked into local variables (`harness`, `model`, `provider`, `modelRef`,\n  `effort`, `local`).\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1374` — `--local` branch takes the inline path; without\n  it the loop submits to the running DDx server.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1384` — `ValidateForExecuteLoop(harness, model, provider, modelRef)`\n  runs before any bead is claimed.\n\nThe flag values eventually reach `ExecuteBead`, which in turn builds\n`RunOptions`:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:62` — `RunOptions` struct definition.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:498` — `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})`\n  constructs the options, including `Provider: opts.Provider` on line 504.\n\n**Observation:** `RouteFlags` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:51`) has a `Provider` field; so does\n`RunOptions` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:74`). They are the raw CLI inputs. The gap is not here\n— it is at the normalization boundary (Layer 2).\n\n## Layer 2 — Normalize flags into `RouteRequest`\n\nThe normalization boundary converts raw CLI flags into the minimal routing ask\nthat the routing engine evaluates.\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:289` — `NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:294` — `RouteRequest{Effort, Permissions}`\n  initial struct. Note the absence of `Provider`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:303` — `flags.Harness != \"\"` branch assigns\n  `HarnessOverride` directly.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` — `flags.Provider != \"\"` branch: the\n  provider flag is **only** consulted as a harness-alias lookup. If\n  `--provider vidar-omlx` does not match any entry in `builtinHarnesses`, the\n  value is silently discarded.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:318` — `flags.Model != \"\"` branch routes the\n  model through `catalog.NormalizeModelRef` to produce either `ModelRef` (known\n  in catalog) or `ModelPin` (exact, bypasses catalog policy).\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` — `RouteRequest` struct definition. Fields:\n  `Profile`, `ModelRef`, `ModelPin`, `Effort`, `Permissions`, `HarnessOverride`.\n  **No `Provider` field — see Gap #1.**\n\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\n`--provider vidar-omlx` does not name a registered harness, so the provider\nflag is dropped at `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305`. `--model-ref qwen/qwen3.6` flows through\n`NormalizeModelRef`; since it is not in the shared catalog, it becomes a\n`ModelPin`. The resulting `RouteRequest` carries only\n`{ModelPin: \"qwen/qwen3.6\"}`.\n\n## Layer 3 — Discovery probe\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:117` — `init()` sets the process-wide cache\n  TTL to `30 * time.Second`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:124` — `DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)` entry.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:125` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:130` — cache hit returns the cached result if still fresh.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:141` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:155` — probes every configured provider via `providerstatus.Probe`; silently skips unreachable ones (partial failure is tolerated).\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:162` — cache write under mutex.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:170` — `InvalidateDiscoveryCache` is the only\n  way to force a re-probe in tests.\n\nDiscovery is invoked on the routing hot-path only when a `ModelPin` is not in\nthe catalog:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:576` — in `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans`, the\n  discovery probe fires when `req.ModelPin != \"\" && req.ModelRef == \"\" && r.Discovery == nil`.\n\nDiscovery is invoked a second time on the dispatch path:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` — in `resolveNativeAgentProvider`,\n  `r.Discovery.ProvidersForModel(model)` looks up which providers advertised\n  the requested model.\n\n## Layer 4 — Candidate planning\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:27` — `BuildCandidatePlans(req, stateOverride)` entry.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:33` through `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:46` — iterates every registered harness. `TestOnly` harnesses (script, virtual) are skipped unless `req.HarnessOverride` names them explicitly — this is the guard that prevents `script` from leaking into standard-tier fallback chains (see ddx-869848ec in the comment on line 40).\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:47` — calls `evaluateCandidate` per harness.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:106` — `evaluateCandidate(name, harness, req, stateOverride)`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` — `rejectIfNotViable(plan, req)` runs the\n  viability gate. Checks:\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:399` — `!s.Installed`.\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:402` — `!s.Reachable`.\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:405` — `!s.Authenticated`.\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:408` — quota exhausted.\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:411` — degraded.\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:414` — policy violation.\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:418` — harness override mismatch.\n\nNote: none of these check *capability* — context window, tool support, effort\nlevel support. That is Gap #6.\n\n## Layer 5 — Fuzzy matching\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:66` — `FuzzyMatchModel(input)` entry.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:70` — delegates to `fuzzyMatchInPool` using\n  `d.AllModels()` (a flat deduplicated pool across all providers — not per-provider).\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` — `fuzzyMatchInPool(input, pool)`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:78` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:82` — exact-match short-circuit.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:89` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:96` — prefix match: candidate `m` is considered if `len(m) > len(input) && m[:len(input)] == input`. **The comparison is case-sensitive and treats slashes literally (Gap #3).**\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:106` — tiebreak: shortest suffix wins; alphabetical on equal suffix length. **Not version-aware (Gap #5).**\n\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\nPool likely contains `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit`. Input `qwen/qwen3.6` fails the\nexact check, then fails the prefix check because `Q != q` and the slash is not\nin the pool entries. The function returns `(\"\", false)`. `resolveNativeAgentProvider`\nfalls through to the `isOpenRouterModel` heuristic at\n`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:544` (vendor/model format → openrouter),\nwhich is not what the operator wanted.\n\n## Layer 6 — Scoring\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:477` — `scoreCandidate(profile, plan)` entry.\n  Returns a `float64`; higher is better.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:478` — base score `100`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:485` — `switch profile` over `cheap`, `standard`, `smart`, default.\n  - `cheap` (lines 486–493): `local` +40, within-quota subscription +20, penalize `cr * 30`.\n  - `standard` (lines 495–502): `local` +25, within-quota +15, penalize `cr * 10`.\n  - `smart` (lines 504–512): `cr * 20` bonus (expensive > cheap), within-quota +5.\n  - default (lines 514–522): treated as standard.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:525` — quota >80%: penalty scales with over-usage.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:528` — quota state unknown: −3.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:531` — routing-signal freshness: `cached` −1, `stale` −4.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:542` — historical success rate (≥3 samples):\n  ≥0.8 +20, <0.5 −30.\n\n**No provider-affinity bonus (Gap #4).** If two harnesses both report viable\nfor the same requested model, `scoreCandidate` does not look at\n`plan.Provider` vs the provider the model was discovered on.\n\n## Layer 7 — Candidate ranking and selection\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:434` — `RankCandidates(profile, plans)`.\n  - Assigns scores (line 444) or `-1` for non-viable (line 441).\n  - Stable sort (lines 448–466): viable before non-viable; descending score;\n    tiebreak prefers local-cost candidates, then alphabetical harness name.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:556` — `SelectBestCandidate(plans)` returns\n  the first viable plan, or `\"no viable harness candidate: all harnesses rejected\"`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:567` — `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans` is the\n  full-probe wrapper used by actual dispatch (it calls live `ProbeHarnessState`\n  per harness before building plans).\n\n## Layer 8 — Dispatch\n\n`Runner.Run` is the dispatch fork:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:141` — `Run(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)` entry.\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:148` — `resolveHarness(opts)` picks the harness.\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:376` — `resolveHarness(opts)` definition.\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:382` — **Gap #2 origin**: if `opts.Harness == \"\"` and\n  `opts.Provider != \"\"`, the `--provider` value is looked up as a harness\n  alias. If it matches a registered harness (e.g. `agent`, `local`), that\n  harness is chosen; otherwise `resolveHarness` falls back to `Config.Harness`.\n  This is a parallel path that bypasses `NormalizeRouteRequest` entirely.\n\nDispatch branches (in order):\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:165` — `harnessName == \"virtual\"` → `RunVirtual(opts)`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:170` — `harnessName == \"agent\"` → `RunAgent(opts)` (embedded).\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:175` — `harnessName == \"script\"` → `RunScript(opts)`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:187` — HTTP-provider harnesses with no binary (`lmstudio`, `openrouter`): surfaces the harness name as `opts.Provider` and delegates to `RunAgent`. The comment on lines 185–186 references `ddx-501e87ef` — the bug where cheap-tier attempts were burning <1s with \"exec: no command\".\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:278` — default exec path: `Executor.ExecuteInDir(ctx, harness.Binary, args, stdin, execDir)`.\n\nEmbedded agent dispatch:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:65` — `RunAgent(opts)`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:94` — `resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider(wd, model, opts.Provider, opts.ModelRef)`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:464` — `resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider` entry.\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:493` — `resolveNativeAgentProvider(workDir, model, explicitProvider, explicitModelRef)`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:519` — **Gap #2 again**: `explicitProvider != \"\"` overrides the default provider with the raw `opts.Provider` string. This is where `--provider vidar-omlx` finally re-enters the flow, but by now the routing engine has already ranked candidates without knowing about it.\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` through `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:548` — discovery-based provider resolution: if the model isn't in the catalog and discovery has results, route to the first provider that advertises the model (exact or fuzzy).\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:555` — `cfg.BuildProviderWithOverrides(providerName, overrides)` materializes the provider object from the native agent config.\n\n## Layer 9 — Wire-level HTTP request\n\nThe embedded agent library builds the request body and dispatches to the\nprovider endpoint. -f \"$DOC_PATH\" ]; then\n    echo \"citations: $DOC_PATH not found\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\n# Match citations of the form `path/to/file.ext:NN` inside backticks.\n# We only validate citations whose path looks like a file (contains a slash\n# and ends with a known extension).\ncitations=$(grep -oE '`[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+\\.(go|ts|tsx|js|yaml|yml|md|sh):[0-9]+`' \"$DOC_PATH\" | tr -d '`' | sort -u)\n\nif [ -z \"$citations\" ]; then\n    echo \"citations: no citations found in $DOC_PATH — fail-closed\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\nfailures=0\ntotal=0\n\nwhile IFS= read -r cite; do\n    [ -z \"$cite\" ] && continue\n    total=$((total + 1))\n    path=\"${cite%:*}\"\n    line=\"${cite##*:}\"\n    if [ ! -f \"$path\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: MISSING FILE: $cite\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\n    # Line count check; awk 'END{print NR}' counts lines portably.\n    nlines=$(awk 'END{print NR}' \"$path\")\n    if [ \"$line\" -gt \"$nlines\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: LINE OUT OF RANGE: $cite (file has $nlines lines)\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\n    # Sanity check: the line must not be blank, since every real citation\n    # points at a struct, func, or statement.\n    content=$(awk -v n=\"$line\" 'NR==n' \"$path\")\n    if [ -z \"$(echo \"$content\" | tr -d '[:space:]')\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: BLANK LINE: $cite\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\ndone <<< \"$citations\"\n\nif [ \"$failures\" -gt 0 ]; then\n    echo \"citations: $failures/$total failed for $DOC_PATH\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\necho \"citations: $total/$total ok for $DOC_PATH\"","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"465c02eb-3d0f-4e0e-9149-057b595f6ab5","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:07.386Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# Verifies every `path:line` citation in SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md\n# points to a file that exists and a line that exists. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GZzJDTKtx6XSP7gKPQq28S","type":"tool_result","content":"282\t//     harness binary receives the model string as-is and can error clearly.\n283\t//     - Otherwise: try catalog resolution:\n284\t//     - If known in catalog: ModelRef.\n285\t//     - If not known: ModelPin (exact pin, bypasses catalog).\n286\t//  3. If flags.Profile is set → Profile.\n287\t//  4. Else fall back to cfg.Model (same catalog resolution) or cfg.Profile.\n288\t//  5. If nothing is set → cfg.Harness becomes HarnessOverride (legacy fallback).\n289\tfunc NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest {\n290\t\tif catalog == nil {\n291\t\t\tcatalog = BuiltinCatalog\n292\t\t}\n293\t\n294\t\treq := RouteRequest{\n295\t\t\tEffort:      flags.Effort,\n296\t\t\tPermissions: flags.Permissions,\n297\t\t}\n298\t\n299\t\t// 1. Harness override constrains routing to one harness.\n300\t\t//    --provider that names a known harness (or alias like \"local\") also\n301\t\t//    pins the harness so the routing engine never falls through to a\n302\t\t//    different harness.\n303\t\tif flags.Harness != \"\" {\n304\t\t\treq.HarnessOverride = flags.Harness\n305\t\t} else if flags.Provider != \"\" {\n306\t\t\tresolved := resolveHarnessAlias(flags.Provider)\n307\t\t\t// Only treat as harness override if it names a registered harness;\n308\t\t\t// otherwise it's a ddx-agent provider name (e.g. \"vidar\") and\n309\t\t\t// should not constrain harness selection.\n310\t\t\tif _, ok := builtinHarnesses[resolved]; ok {\n311\t\t\t\treq.HarnessOverride = resolved"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01BpB5ybRXLsC8V3pvMEmJe9","session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"3708917d-10f5-4047-87a8-eca15bf792b1","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:52.477Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FbNtbFVUbDesKpUsBMMxbR","type":"tool_result","content":"470\t\n471\t// scoreCandidate returns a score for a viable candidate given the profile.\n472\t// Higher is better.\n473\t//\n474\t// Routing priority policy:\n475\t//   - cheap/standard: local (free, no quota) > subscription-within-quota > pay-per-token\n476\t//   - smart: cloud capability wins; local models are typically quantized and lower-quality\n477\tfunc scoreCandidate(profile string, plan CandidatePlan) float64 {\n478\t\tbase := 100.0\n479\t\n480\t\tcr := costClassRank[plan.CostClass] // 0=local,1=cheap,2=medium,3=expensive\n481\t\n482\t\t// withinQuota is true when a subscription harness has headroom (cost already sunk).\n483\t\twithinQuota := plan.IsSubscription && (plan.State.QuotaOK || plan.State.QuotaState == \"ok\")\n484\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01BpB5ybRXLsC8V3pvMEmJe9","session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"93141aca-fb97-4955-80e0-dc1986fc1d6f","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:53.965Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Ptz1X4kq7Wdpagw4Hv9VmL","type":"tool_result","content":"diff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..3438eff3\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go\n@@ -0,0 +1,230 @@\n+package agent\n+\n+import (\n+\t\"bufio\"\n+\t\"encoding/json\"\n+\t\"io\"\n+\t\"net/http\"\n+\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n+\t\"os\"\n+\t\"path/filepath\"\n+\t\"strings\"\n+\t\"testing\"\n+\t\"time\"\n+\n+\t\"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent/provider/openai\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n+)\n+\n+// omlxFixture is the parsed representation of a testdata/omlx-wire/*.jsonl file.\n+// Each fixture captures a recorded vidar-omlx SSE response plus the outcome the\n+// consumer must produce when it replays. Fixtures are authored either by hand\n+// (the checked-in happy/error paths) or regenerated by\n+// scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh against a live omlx endpoint.\n+type omlxFixture struct {\n+\tMeta   omlxFixtureMeta\n+\tFrames []string // raw SSE bodies, written verbatim in order\n+}\n+\n+type omlxFixtureMeta struct {\n+\tName        string              `json:\"name\"`\n+\tDescription string              `json:\"description\"`\n+\tExpected    omlxFixtureExpected `json:\"expected\"`\n+}\n+\n+type omlxFixtureExpected struct {\n+\t// Status is \"success\" or \"error\".\n+\tStatus string `json:\"status\"`\n+\t// ErrorMustNotContain is a substring that the stream error — if any — must\n+\t// not contain. This is the regression guard: \"unexpected end of JSON input\"\n+\t// is the exact signature of the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame defect (bead\n+\t// agent-f237e07b) that v0.3.14's sseCommentFilter fixed.\n+\tErrorMustNotContain string `json:\"error_must_not_contain\"`\n+\t// ContentContains is a substring the assembled content must contain on the\n+\t// happy path.\n+\tContentContains string `json:\"content_contains\"`\n+\t// FinishReason is the expected finish reason on the happy path.\n+\tFinishReason string `json:\"finish_reason\"`\n+\t// ErrorContains is a substring the surfaced error must contain on the\n+\t// error path — asserts the error is propagated rather than swallowed.\n+\tErrorContains string `json:\"error_contains\"`\n+}\n+\n+// loadOMLXFixture reads a JSONL fixture from testdata/omlx-wire/. The first\n+// line is the {\"kind\":\"meta\", ...} record; remaining {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...}\n+// lines are the SSE bodies to stream in order.\n+func loadOMLXFixture(t *testing.T, path string) omlxFixture {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tf, err := os.Open(path) // #nosec G304 -- test fixtures are checked in.\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"open fixture %s\", path)\n+\tt.Cleanup(func() { _ = f.Close() })\n+\n+\tvar fix omlxFixture\n+\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)\n+\t// Default 64KB scanner buffer is tight for long reasoning lines.\n+\tscanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 1024*1024)\n+\tfirst := true\n+\tfor scanner.Scan() {\n+\t\tline := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())\n+\t\tif line == \"\" {\n+\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tvar record struct {\n+\t\t\tKind string          `json:\"kind\"`\n+\t\t\tBody string          `json:\"body\"`\n+\t\t\tRaw  json.RawMessage `json:\"-\"`\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {\n+\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"parsing fixture line in %s: %v\", path, err)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tswitch record.Kind {\n+\t\tcase \"meta\":\n+\t\t\tif !first {\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: meta record must be the first non-empty line\", path)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &fix.Meta); err != nil {\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"parsing meta in %s: %v\", path, err)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\tcase \"frame\":\n+\t\t\tfix.Frames = append(fix.Frames, record.Body)\n+\t\tdefault:\n+\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: unknown record kind %q\", path, record.Kind)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tfirst = false\n+\t}\n+\trequire.NoError(t, scanner.Err(), \"scanning fixture %s\", path)\n+\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Meta.Name, \"fixture %s: missing meta.name\", path)\n+\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Frames, \"fixture %s: no frame records\", path)\n+\treturn fix\n+}\n+\n+// newOMLXReplayServer stands up an httptest.Server that behaves like a vidar-omlx\n+// /v1/chat/completions endpoint by replaying the fixture's frames verbatim,\n+// with a short inter-frame flush so the consumer's SSE decoder sees per-chunk\n+// arrivals rather than one batched blob.\n+func newOMLXReplayServer(t *testing.T, frames []string) *httptest.Server {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tsrv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n+\t\t// Match omlx's advertised Content-Type exactly — the SSE comment-frame\n+\t\t// middleware in provider/openai activates only when Content-Type starts\n+\t\t// with text/event-stream.\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream; charset=utf-8\")\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\")\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Transfer-Encoding\", \"chunked\")\n+\t\tflusher, _ := w.(http.Flusher)\n+\t\tfor _, body := range frames {\n+\t\t\tif _, err := io.WriteString(w, body); err != nil {\n+\t\t\t\treturn\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tif flusher != nil {\n+\t\t\t\tflusher.Flush()\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\t// Small delay to reproduce per-frame arrival; long enough that the\n+\t\t\t// decoder cannot batch frames, short enough that the test stays\n+\t\t\t// under a second total.\n+\t\t\ttime.Sleep(5 * time.Millisecond)\n+\t\t}\n+\t}))\n+\tt.Cleanup(srv.Close)\n+\treturn srv\n+}\n+\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd drives a full ddx work orchestration\n+// path (Runner.RunAgent → agentlib.Run → openai.Provider.ChatStream) against an\n+// httptest double that replays recorded omlx SSE wire traces. This is the\n+// consumer-side smoke test for ddx-agent v0.3.14 — see bead ddx-bbb2d177.\n+//\n+// What this guards against:\n+//\n+//  1. A future ddx-agent bump reintroducing the SSE comment-frame bug (bead\n+//     agent-f237e07b): the happy-path fixture includes `: keep-alive\\n\\n`\n+//     frames between data frames and must NOT surface \"unexpected end of\n+//     JSON input\".\n+//  2. A DDx-side change breaking the omlx dispatch path (e.g., a header or\n+//     request-shape change that trips omlx's parser).\n+//  3. A silent-success regression where the stream is consumed without error\n+//     but the assembled content is empty or malformed — the happy-path assertion\n+//     pins both the final content and finish_reason.\n+//  4. Error-path regressions where a real model-side failure is swallowed by\n+//     the consumer — the error-path fixture emits a malformed data frame and\n+//     asserts the consumer surfaces the error with a specific signature (and\n+//     specifically NOT \"unexpected end of JSON input\", which would mean the\n+//     SSE filter regressed).\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd(t *testing.T) {\n+\tfixtureDir := filepath.Join(\"testdata\", \"omlx-wire\")\n+\tcases := []string{\"happy_path.jsonl\", \"error_path.jsonl\"}\n+\n+\tfor _, name := range cases {\n+\t\tname := name\n+\t\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\n+\t\t\tfix := loadOMLXFixture(t, filepath.Join(fixtureDir, name))\n+\n+\t\t\tsrv := newOMLXReplayServer(t, fix.Frames)\n+\n+\t\t\tprovider := openai.New(openai.Config{\n+\t\t\t\tBaseURL: srv.URL + \"/v1\",\n+\t\t\t\tAPIKey:  \"test\",\n+\t\t\t\tModel:   \"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\",\n+\t\t\t\t// Flavor \"omlx\" pins the capability table to the one used in\n+\t\t\t\t// production for vidar-omlx routing — matches the real\n+\t\t\t\t// dispatch path.\n+\t\t\t\tFlavor: \"omlx\",\n+\t\t\t})\n+\n+\t\t\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir(), TimeoutMS: 10000})\n+\t\t\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\n+\t\t\tr.AgentProvider = provider\n+\n+\t\t\t// A WorkDir prevents the agent from using the live worktree when\n+\t\t\t// it invokes its built-in read/write/bash tools during the loop.\n+\t\t\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\n+\t\t\t\tHarness: \"agent\",\n+\t\t\t\tPrompt:  \"smoke test — omlx fixture replay\",\n+\t\t\t\tWorkDir: t.TempDir(),\n+\t\t\t})\n+\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"RunAgent must not return a plumbing error; failures are reported via result.ExitCode / result.Error\")\n+\n+\t\t\t// The regression signature is always disallowed — on BOTH paths.\n+\t\t\t// v0.3.13 would surface this even for well-formed streams when\n+\t\t\t// keep-alive comment frames were present.\n+\t\t\tif fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\"consumer surfaced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature — the sseCommentFilter in ddx-agent v0.3.14+ is missing or broken\")\n+\t\t\t}\n+\n+\t\t\tswitch fix.Meta.Expected.Status {\n+\t\t\tcase \"success\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \"happy-path fixture must close cleanly; got error=%q\", result.Error)\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Empty(t, result.Error, \"happy-path must produce no error\")\n+\t\t\t\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ContentContains; want != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, result.Output, want,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\"happy-path must assemble content from post-keep-alive data frames\")\n+\t\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tcase \"error\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, \"error-path fixture must report failure via ExitCode=1\")\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, \"error-path must surface a non-empty error\")\n+\t\t\t\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorContains; The full replay test\n+// above would also fail in that case, but a dedicated assertion surfaces the\n+// structural problem without the noise of a streaming-test stack trace.\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist(t *testing.T) {\n+\tfor _, name := range []string{\"happy_path.jsonl\", \"error_path.jsonl\"} {\n+\t\tp := filepath.Join(\"testdata\", \"omlx-wire\", name)\n+\t\tinfo, err := os.Stat(p)\n+\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"missing fixture %s\", p)\n+\t\tassert.False(t, info.IsDir(), \"%s must be a file\", p)\n+\t\tassert.NotZero(t, info.Size(), \"%s must not be empty\", p)\n+\t}\n+}\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..fd43c76e\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\n@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@\n+//go:build omlx_smoke\n+\n+// Opt-in live smoke test for the ddx work → ddx-agent → vidar-omlx consumer\n+// path. TestOMLXLiveSmoke(t *testing.T) {\n+\tbaseURL := os.Getenv(\"OMLX_SMOKE_URL\")\n+\tif baseURL == \"\" {\n+\t\tt.Skip(\"OMLX_SMOKE_URL not set — live smoke requires an omlx endpoint\")\n+\t}\n+\tmodel produced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature\")\n+\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \"live omlx smoke must close cleanly; error=%q\", result.Error)\n+\tassert.NotEmpty(t, 00000000..1f3cbdfa\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@\n+# omlx SSE Wire Fixtures\n+\n+These fixtures drive `cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go` — the consumer-side\n+end-to-end smoke test that guards `ddx work` against regressions on the\n+`ddx-agent → vidar-omlx` dispatch path. Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which\n+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead\n+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).\n+\n+## File format\n+\n+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:\n+\n+- First line: `{\"kind\":\"meta\", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion\n+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or\n+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).\n+- Remaining lines: `{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"<raw SSE body>\"}` — one frame per\n+  record, written verbatim (including `\\n\\n` terminators) to the test HTTP\n+  response in arrival order.\n+\n+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that\n+triggered the v0.3.13 \"unexpected end of JSON input\" regression) and normal\n+`data: {...}` chunks.\n+\n+## When to regenerate\n+\n+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the\n+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase\n+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. Do NOT regenerate\n+just to refresh timestamps.\n+\n+## How to regenerate\n+\n+Use `scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh`, which wraps `ddx agent run` with\n+`AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1` (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14) so the full\n+stream is captured to a sidecar JSONL, then reshapes it into the fixture\n+format:\n+\n+```bash\n+scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+  --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+  --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+  --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+  --name     happy-path \\\n+  --status   success \\\n+  --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+```\n+\n+After the script writes the fixture, hand-edit the `meta.expected` block to\n+pin the `content_contains` / `error_contains` / `finish_reason` assertions for\n+the capture you just took — the script cannot infer those from the wire\n+bytes alone.\n+\n+## Why fixtures live here instead of the upstream agent repo\n+\n+The agent repo (`DocumentDrivenDX/agent`) has its own provider-level SSE\n+tests in `provider/openai/openai_test.go`. Those guard the SDK's handling of\n+SSE framing in isolation. The fixtures in *this* directory guard the DDx\n+consumer — `ddx work` → `Runner.RunAgent` → `agentlib.Run` — against both\n+SDK regressions (a bad ddx-agent bump) and DDx-side regressions (a header or\n+request-shape change that trips omlx's parser). The two layers cover\n+different failure modes and both matter.\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..3dd1154f\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl\n@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@\n+{\"kind\":\"meta\",\"name\":\"error-path\",\"description\":\"omlx-style server sends role delta, a reasoning chunk, then a malformed JSON data frame. b/scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh\n@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@\n+#!/usr/bin/env bash\n+# capture-omlx-fixture.sh — regenerate a testdata/omlx-wire fixture from a\n+# live omlx endpoint. Wraps `ddx agent run` with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1\n+# (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14 — bead agent-f237e07b) and reshapes the wire\n+# dump into the fixture JSONL format the consumer-side smoke test expects.\n+#\n+# Usage:\n+#   scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+#     --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+#     --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+#     --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+#     --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+#\n+# Required tools: ddx (v0.6.0-alpha22+), jq.\n+#\n+# What the script does:\n+#   1. Runs an isolated ddx agent invocation against the given omlx endpoint\n+#      with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 so the whole\n+#      SSE stream is captured to a sidecar wire file.\n+#   2. Extracts every `dir==\"response\"` body chunk in arrival order and emits\n+#      one {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...} record per chunk.\n+#   3. Prepends a {\"kind\":\"meta\",...} record with the endpoint, model, prompt,\n+#      and a user-supplied expected outcome so the replay test knows what to\n+#      assert.\n+#\n+# Fixtures are checked in. Regenerate only when the omlx server changes wire\n+# behavior (new frame type, header shift, chunk-size change) in a way the\n+# existing fixtures no longer represent.\n+\n+set -euo pipefail\n+\n+endpoint=\"\"\n+model=\"\"\n+prompt=\"\"\n+out=\"\"\n+name=\"\"\n+status=\"success\"\n+\n+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n+  case \"$1\" in\n+    --endpoint) endpoint=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --model)    model=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --prompt)   prompt=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --out)      out=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --name)     name=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --status)   status=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;  # success | error\n+    -h|--help)\n+      sed -n '2,28p' \"$0\"\n+      exit 0\n+      ;;\n+    *) echo \"unknown arg: $1\" >&2; exit 2 ;;\n+  esac\n+done\n+\n+for v in endpoint model prompt out; do\n+  if [[ -z \"${!v}\" ]]; then\n+    echo \"missing required --$v\" >&2\n+    exit 2\n+  fi\n+done\n+\n+command -v jq >/dev/null || { echo \"jq is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+command -v ddx >/dev/null || { echo \"ddx is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+\n+wire_file=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-wire.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\"' EXIT\n+\n+echo \"capturing wire dump from $endpoint → $wire_file\" >&2\n+\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_FILE=\"$wire_file\" \\\n+ddx agent run \\\n+  --harness agent \\\n+  --provider vidar-omlx \\\n+  --model \"$model\" \\\n+  --prompt-text \"$prompt\" \\\n+  --timeout 120s \\\n+  >/dev/null || {\n+    echo \"ddx agent run failed — fixture capture incomplete\" >&2\n+    echo \"wire dump (may still be useful): $wire_file\" >&2\n+    exit 1\n+  }\n+\n+[[ -z \"$name\" ]] && name=\"$(basename \"$out\" .jsonl)\"\n+\n+tmp_out=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-fixture.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\" \"$tmp_out\"' EXIT\n+\n+# Meta record — expected outcome is a hint for the replay test. Authors may\n+# need to hand-edit content_contains / error_contains after inspecting the\n+# captured body.\n+jq -cn \\\n+  --arg name \"$name\" \\\n+  --arg desc \"Captured $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) from $endpoint model=$model prompt=$prompt\" \\\n+  --arg status \"$status\" \\\n+  '{kind:\"meta\", name:$name, description:$desc,\n+    expected:{status:$status, error_must_not_contain:\"unexpected end of JSON input\"}}' \\\n+  >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+# Frame records — every response chunk in capture order. AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE\n+# with STREAM_FULL=1 emits one {\"dir\":\"response\",\"body\":\"…\"} record per read.\n+jq -c 'select(.dir==\"response\" and (.body|type==\"string\") and (.body|length>0))\n+       | {kind:\"frame\", body:.body}' \\\n+  \"$wire_file\" >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+mv \"$tmp_out\" \"$out\"\n+echo \"wrote $(wc -l < \"$out\") records to $out\" >&2","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"51e05227-4794-4fdf-b297-f2720148e2a0","uuid":"ad85bb2f-b87a-4555-85d6-b2e11af2638d","timestamp":"2026-04-18T04:31:26.286Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"diff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..3438eff3\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go\n@@ -0,0 +1,230 @@\n+package agent\n+\n+import (\n+\t\"bufio\"\n+\t\"encoding/json\"\n+\t\"io\"\n+\t\"net/http\"\n+\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n+\t\"os\"\n+\t\"path/filepath\"\n+\t\"strings\"\n+\t\"testing\"\n+\t\"time\"\n+\n+\t\"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent/provider/openai\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n+)\n+\n+// omlxFixture is the parsed representation of a testdata/omlx-wire/*.jsonl file.\n+// Each fixture captures a recorded vidar-omlx SSE response plus the outcome the\n+// consumer must produce when it replays. Fixtures are authored either by hand\n+// (the checked-in happy/error paths) or regenerated by\n+// scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh against a live omlx endpoint.\n+type omlxFixture struct {\n+\tMeta   omlxFixtureMeta\n+\tFrames []string // raw SSE bodies, written verbatim in order\n+}\n+\n+type omlxFixtureMeta struct {\n+\tName        string              `json:\"name\"`\n+\tDescription string              `json:\"description\"`\n+\tExpected    omlxFixtureExpected `json:\"expected\"`\n+}\n+\n+type omlxFixtureExpected struct {\n+\t// Status is \"success\" or \"error\".\n+\tStatus string `json:\"status\"`\n+\t// ErrorMustNotContain is a substring that the stream error — if any — must\n+\t// not contain. This is the regression guard: \"unexpected end of JSON input\"\n+\t// is the exact signature of the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame defect (bead\n+\t// agent-f237e07b) that v0.3.14's sseCommentFilter fixed.\n+\tErrorMustNotContain string `json:\"error_must_not_contain\"`\n+\t// ContentContains is a substring the assembled content must contain on the\n+\t// happy path.\n+\tContentContains string `json:\"content_contains\"`\n+\t// FinishReason is the expected finish reason on the happy path.\n+\tFinishReason string `json:\"finish_reason\"`\n+\t// ErrorContains is a substring the surfaced error must contain on the\n+\t// error path — asserts the error is propagated rather than swallowed.\n+\tErrorContains string `json:\"error_contains\"`\n+}\n+\n+// loadOMLXFixture reads a JSONL fixture from testdata/omlx-wire/. The first\n+// line is the {\"kind\":\"meta\", ...} record; remaining {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...}\n+// lines are the SSE bodies to stream in order.\n+func loadOMLXFixture(t *testing.T, path string) omlxFixture {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tf, err := os.Open(path) // #nosec G304 -- test fixtures are checked in.\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"open fixture %s\", path)\n+\tt.Cleanup(func() { _ = f.Close() })\n+\n+\tvar fix omlxFixture\n+\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)\n+\t// Default 64KB scanner buffer is tight for long reasoning lines.\n+\tscanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 1024*1024)\n+\tfirst := true\n+\tfor scanner.Scan() {\n+\t\tline := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())\n+\t\tif line == \"\" {\n+\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tvar record struct {\n+\t\t\tKind string          `json:\"kind\"`\n+\t\t\tBody string          `json:\"body\"`\n+\t\t\tRaw  json.RawMessage `json:\"-\"`\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {\n+\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"parsing fixture line in %s: %v\", path, err)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tswitch record.Kind {\n+\t\tcase \"meta\":\n+\t\t\tif !first {\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: meta record must be the first non-empty line\", path)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &fix.Meta); err != nil {\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"parsing meta in %s: %v\", path, err)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\tcase \"frame\":\n+\t\t\tfix.Frames = append(fix.Frames, record.Body)\n+\t\tdefault:\n+\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: unknown record kind %q\", path, record.Kind)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tfirst = false\n+\t}\n+\trequire.NoError(t, scanner.Err(), \"scanning fixture %s\", path)\n+\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Meta.Name, \"fixture %s: missing meta.name\", path)\n+\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Frames, \"fixture %s: no frame records\", path)\n+\treturn fix\n+}\n+\n+// newOMLXReplayServer stands up an httptest.Server that behaves like a vidar-omlx\n+// /v1/chat/completions endpoint by replaying the fixture's frames verbatim,\n+// with a short inter-frame flush so the consumer's SSE decoder sees per-chunk\n+// arrivals rather than one batched blob.\n+func newOMLXReplayServer(t *testing.T, frames []string) *httptest.Server {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tsrv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n+\t\t// Match omlx's advertised Content-Type exactly — the SSE comment-frame\n+\t\t// middleware in provider/openai activates only when Content-Type starts\n+\t\t// with text/event-stream.\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream; charset=utf-8\")\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\")\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Transfer-Encoding\", \"chunked\")\n+\t\tflusher, _ := w.(http.Flusher)\n+\t\tfor _, body := range frames {\n+\t\t\tif _, err := io.WriteString(w, body); failures are reported via result.ExitCode / result.Error\")\n+\n+\t\t\t// The regression signature is always disallowed — on BOTH paths.\n+\t\t\t// v0.3.13 would surface this even for well-formed streams when\n+\t\t\t// keep-alive comment frames were present.\n+\t\t\tif fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\"consumer surfaced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature — the sseCommentFilter in ddx-agent v0.3.14+ is missing or broken\")\n+\t\t\t}\n+\n+\t\t\tswitch fix.Meta.Expected.Status {\n+\t\t\tcase \"success\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \"happy-path fixture must close cleanly; the consumer-side\n+end-to-end smoke test that guards `ddx work` against regressions on the\n+`ddx-agent → vidar-omlx` dispatch path. Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which\n+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead\n+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).\n+\n+## File format\n+\n+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:\n+\n+- First line: `{\"kind\":\"meta\", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion\n+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or\n+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).\n+- Remaining lines: `{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"<raw SSE body>\"}` — one frame per\n+  record, written verbatim (including `\\n\\n` terminators) to the test HTTP\n+  response in arrival order.\n+\n+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that\n+triggered the v0.3.13 \"unexpected end of JSON input\" regression) and normal\n+`data: {...}` chunks.\n+\n+## When to regenerate\n+\n+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the\n+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase\n+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. Do NOT regenerate\n+just to refresh timestamps.\n+\n+## How to regenerate\n+\n+Use `scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh`, which wraps `ddx agent run` with\n+`AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1` (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14) so the full\n+stream is captured to a sidecar JSONL, then reshapes it into the fixture\n+format:\n+\n+```bash\n+scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+  --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+  --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+  --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+  --name     happy-path \\\n+  --status   success \\\n+  --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+```\n+\n+After the script writes the fixture, hand-edit the `meta.expected` block to\n+pin the `content_contains` / `error_contains` / `finish_reason` assertions for\n+the capture you just took — the script cannot infer those from the wire\n+bytes alone.\n+\n+## Why fixtures live here instead of the upstream agent repo\n+\n+The agent repo (`DocumentDrivenDX/agent`) has its own provider-level SSE\n+tests in `provider/openai/openai_test.go`. Those guard the SDK's handling of\n+SSE framing in isolation. The fixtures in *this* directory guard the DDx\n+consumer — `ddx work` → `Runner.RunAgent` → `agentlib.Run` — against both\n+SDK regressions (a bad ddx-agent bump) and DDx-side regressions (a header or\n+request-shape change that trips omlx's parser). b/scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh\n@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@\n+#!/usr/bin/env bash\n+# capture-omlx-fixture.sh — regenerate a testdata/omlx-wire fixture from a\n+# live omlx endpoint. Wraps `ddx agent run` with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1\n+# (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14 — bead agent-f237e07b) and reshapes the wire\n+# dump into the fixture JSONL format the consumer-side smoke test expects.\n+#\n+# Usage:\n+#   scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+#     --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+#     --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+#     --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+#     --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+#\n+# Required tools: ddx (v0.6.0-alpha22+), jq.\n+#\n+# What the script does:\n+#   1. Runs an isolated ddx agent invocation against the given omlx endpoint\n+#      with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 so the whole\n+#      SSE stream is captured to a sidecar wire file.\n+#   2. Extracts every `dir==\"response\"` body chunk in arrival order and emits\n+#      one {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...} record per chunk.\n+#   3. Prepends a {\"kind\":\"meta\",...} record with the endpoint, model, prompt,\n+#      and a user-supplied expected outcome so the replay test knows what to\n+#      assert.\n+#\n+# Fixtures are checked in. Regenerate only when the omlx server changes wire\n+# behavior (new frame type, header shift, chunk-size change) in a way the\n+# existing fixtures no longer represent.\n+\n+set -euo pipefail\n+\n+endpoint=\"\"\n+model=\"\"\n+prompt=\"\"\n+out=\"\"\n+name=\"\"\n+status=\"success\"\n+\n+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n+  case \"$1\" in\n+    --endpoint) endpoint=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --model)    model=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --prompt)   prompt=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --out)      out=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --name)     name=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --status)   status=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;  # success | error\n+    -h|--help)\n+      sed -n '2,28p' \"$0\"\n+      exit 0\n+      ;;\n+    *) echo \"unknown arg: $1\" >&2; exit 2 ;;\n+  esac\n+done\n+\n+for v in endpoint model prompt out; do\n+  if [[ -z \"${!v}\" ]]; then\n+    echo \"missing required --$v\" >&2\n+    exit 2\n+  fi\n+done\n+\n+command -v jq >/dev/null || { echo \"jq is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+command -v ddx >/dev/null || { echo \"ddx is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+\n+wire_file=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-wire.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\"' EXIT\n+\n+echo \"capturing wire dump from $endpoint → $wire_file\" >&2\n+\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_FILE=\"$wire_file\" \\\n+ddx agent run \\\n+  --harness agent \\\n+  --provider vidar-omlx \\\n+  --model \"$model\" \\\n+  --prompt-text \"$prompt\" \\\n+  --timeout 120s \\\n+  >/dev/null || {\n+    echo \"ddx agent run failed — fixture capture incomplete\" >&2\n+    echo \"wire dump (may still be useful): $wire_file\" >&2\n+    exit 1\n+  }\n+\n+[[ -z \"$name\" ]] && name=\"$(basename \"$out\" .jsonl)\"\n+\n+tmp_out=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-fixture.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\" \"$tmp_out\"' EXIT\n+\n+# Meta record — expected outcome is a hint for the replay test. Authors may\n+# need to hand-edit content_contains / error_contains after inspecting the\n+# captured body.\n+jq -cn \\\n+  --arg name \"$name\" \\\n+  --arg desc \"Captured $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) from $endpoint model=$model prompt=$prompt\" \\\n+  --arg status \"$status\" \\\n+  '{kind:\"meta\", name:$name, description:$desc,\n+    expected:{status:$status, error_must_not_contain:\"unexpected end of JSON input\"}}' \\\n+  >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+# Frame records — every response chunk in capture order. .ddx/attachments/ddx-bbb65768/events.jsonl:1:{"actor":"erik","body":"2026-04-18 dogfooding evidence: 2 omlx attempts at cheap tier, 2 no_changes.

Run 1 — ddx-91bc770a (Checkpoint dirty parent trees before managed bead execution):
- harness: agent / provider: vidar-omlx / model: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit
- duration: 1300s (21.7 min)
- tokens: 254,413
- outcome: task_no_changes — no rationale written
- bead-class: substantial git-layer refactor

Run 2 — ddx-e3493010 (Resolution Path developer guide):
- harness: agent / provider: vidar-omlx / model: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit
- duration: 4150s (69.2 min)
- tokens: 2,086,838 (2.09M)
- outcome: task_no_changes — no rationale written
- bead-class: documentation-only markdown authoring with code-walk citations

Findings:

1. v0.3.14 SSE filter is solid. 2M+ tokens streamed on run 2 with zero parse errors. Cheap-tier dispatch path no longer the blocker.

2. Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit is not strong enough to reliably close real DDx beads. Both attempts burned significant wall-clock and tokens without committing. "Free" local compute has real opportunity cost when model thrashes for an hour without output.

3. Without ladder escalation, operator has to manually fall back. This IS the motivating case for ddx-bbb65768: default profile should detect no_changes on first tier and escalate to standard (codex subscription) for a second try.

4. AC #5 of this bead sets 50% close-at-cheap target over 10 representative beads. Current observed rate: 0% close-at-cheap over 2 representative beads. Epic delivery must LIFT this rate — either through better capability gating OR by making escalation cheap enough that failed cheap attempts don't dominate total cost.

5. Qwen3-Coder-Next-MLX-4bit might do better on code-heavy beads; gemma-4-31B-it on docs. Worth follow-up experiment pinning different local models per bead-class after autorouting foundation lands.

========================================================

Status: AC7 already satisfied on main; no further code change needed in
this attempt.

What is done
------------
A prior attempt landed commit efbed403 ("feat(agent): add prompt_sha to
manifest + prompt-size CI fixture [ddx-bffb23ae]") which is an ancestor
of HEAD/main in this worktree. Verified with `git merge-base HEAD
efbed403` → efbed403. That commit delivers every AC7 deliverable:

1. prompt_sha in manifest.json for every attempt
   - Field defined: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:177-181
     (`PromptSHA string \`json:"prompt_sha,omitempty"\``)
   - Populated on write: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1127
     (`PromptSHA: promptSHA(promptContent)`)
   - Helper: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1467-1470
     (`promptSHA` returns hex sha256 of the rendered prompt bytes)
   - Regression test: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go

.ddx/attachments/ddx-c7d9054d/events.jsonl:4:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead ddx-c7d9054d — no changes made.

What is done
------------
- Verified the worktree contents: this is the DDx repo (cli/, library/, website/, docs/), not the HELIX repo.
- Searched for the symptom strings and harness logic referenced in the bead description:
    - `REVIEW_STATUS` — only appears in `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (the bead itself).
    - `review output missing` — only appears in `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
    - `helix-run` / `helix.run` — no Go matches in this tree.
- Confirmed by repository layout (CLAUDE.md three-project stack note) that HELIX lives in a separate repo at `~/Projects/helix`. DDx provides primitives (agent service, bead tracker); HELIX owns supervisory loop, phase enforcement, and the helix-run review step that emits the "review output missing REVIEW_STATUS trailer" log line.

What is blocking
----------------
The bead's acceptance criteria are framed entirely in terms of the helix harness behavior:
  (a) "review step succeeds (rc=0, REVIEW_STATUS trailer present, structured verdict captured by harness) on a fresh helix run cycle... with --agent claude", or
  (b) "the review-trailer requirement is removed from the harness and salvage becomes the explicit primary path".
Both options require modifying code in the HELIX repository — specifically the helix-run cycle driver, its review-prompt template, and its REVIEW_STATUS regex/parser. None of that code exists in this DDx worktree, so there is no edit I can make here that would satisfy the AC.

The `ddx agent run` invocation visible in the log lines (rc=0 exit=1 elapsed=350s tokens=0) is the DDx side of the call. The "rc=1" the bead complains about is helix interpreting the agent transcript and failing to find the trailer it asked for; that interpretation, the prompt that asked for the trailer, and the salvage fallback all live in helix.

What a follow-up attempt would need
-----------------------------------
- Re-route this bead to the HELIX repository's tracker, OR
- Re-scope the bead to a DDx-side concern. Plausible DDx-side angles, only if explicitly desired:
    * `ddx agent run`'s `exit=1 rc=0` mapping (cli/internal/agent or cli/cmd/agent_run.go) — clarify what the inner exit code means so helix can distinguish "agent never produced output" (tokens=0) from "agent ran but verdict was missing".
    * Add a structured machine-readable result file emitted by `ddx agent run` so consumers like helix don't need to scrape stdout for a trailer at all.
  These are hypotheses, not the asked-for fix — they would need product agreement before being executed, since they change the DDx/HELIX interface contract.
- If the intent really is to fix the helix harness, run this bead inside an execution worktree of the helix repo (`~/Projects/helix`) where the review-prompt template, the trailer parser, and the salvage path actually exist.

No commit was made. The execution evidence directory is preserved.

.ddx/executions/20260418T011805-e2b15830/prompt.md:17:You are a coding agent executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. You have tools: read, write, edit, bash, ls, grep, find. Use them directly — do not shell out to cat/tail/rg/find; use the tools.

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract. Every AC checkbox must be satisfied by code you write in this pass.

## Process

1. **Read first.** Before writing any code, read the files the bead description names. If the description says "A1 landed X at <path>", read that path. If it references a spec section, read that section. Do NOT start editing without seeing the existing code — this is how you avoid making the same change twice or breaking an invariant you did not know about.

2. **Plan the work briefly.** Internally note which files you will touch, which tests you will add, and which verification commands you will run. Keep the plan short — one or two sentences.

3. **Implement.** Use `edit` for targeted changes to existing files and `write` for brand-new files. Use `read` (not `bash: cat`) to read files. Use `grep` (not `bash: rg`) to search. Use `ls` (not `bash: ls`) for directory listings.

4. **Verify before committing.** Run the project's test and lint commands — typically `cargo test -p <crate>` and `cargo clippy -p <crate> -- -D warnings` for Rust, `bun test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description will usually name the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If anything fails, diagnose and fix it before committing.

5. **Commit exactly once.** Use `git add <specific-paths>` with the file paths you actually changed — never `git add -A`, which can pick up unrelated files in the worktree. Use a conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Commit implementation and tests in the same commit so the reviewer sees them together.

6. **Stop after the commit succeeds.** Return immediately. Do not continue reading files, running extra tests, or asking yourself follow-up questions — the work is done, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success to the orchestrator. Continuing past the commit risks runaway loops.

## If you cannot finish

Write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260418T011805-e2b15830/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up would need. Do NOT commit partial or red code — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, every BLOCKING finding in it is a concrete thing the previous attempt missed. Address each one before declaring the work complete — do not declare no_changes with blocking findings still unaddressed.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new files, write them. Conservative rules (YAGNI, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not touch CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.
- Stage only the files you intentionally changed.

.ddx/executions/20260418T005017-ac81ac97/prompt.md:17:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260418T005017-ac81ac97/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.

bead with kind=reopen summary="review: BLOCK" and kind=execute-bead body:

  post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)
  tier=smart probe_result=ok
  result_rev=23c7a657 base_rev=38c6c64f

No findings body. No per-AC verdict. No diff citation. No ".ddx/executions/"
entry for the review run (only the implementation attempts have execution
directories). No entry in .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl for the review
session. The bead's notes field was set to "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" — empty body.

Result: the operator cannot tell what the reviewer objected to, or whether
the block was substantive. On a 42-minute, ~11M-token execution, the block
is a hard operator blocker because the follow-on attempt would have no
review context to address.

Contract target: a BLOCK verdict must ship with reviewer-authored evidence
substantial enough that a subsequent attempt could address the finding
without a human disambiguating first. PASS verdicts can be evidence-thin;
BLOCK cannot.",

.ddx/executions/20260417T171843-ae5de58e/prompt.md:17:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260417T171843-ae5de58e/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.

.ddx/executions/20260417T182339-81f075dd/manifest.json:14:    "description": "The embedded ddx-agent harness honors the per-run SessionLogDir override (agent_runner.go:136-138: `logDir := opts.SessionLogDir; if empty fall back to r.Config.SessionLogDir`) so that execute-bead runs drop their JSONL trace inside the bundle at .ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl.

The claude harness does NOT honor that override. claude_stream.go:327 reads only r.Config.SessionLogDir, so every claude execute-bead run writes its trace into the runner-wide default (`.ddx/agent-logs/`) and the bundle's embedded/ dir stays empty. execute_bead.go:446 already passes SessionLogDir: embeddedStateDir through RunOptions; it just isn't used by the claude path.

Empirical evidence from axon's 2026-04-15 ADR-018 session: 2 runs with `harness: agent` produced 34-51 MB traces in embedded/; 23 runs with `harness: claude` left embedded/ empty while `.ddx/agent-logs/` grew to 162 MB.

Fix: plumb opts.SessionLogDir into runClaudeStreaming (either accept it as a parameter or read it off resolvedOpts) and prefer it over r.Config.SessionLogDir when non-empty. Matches the embedded harness behavior exactly.

Parity matters: users want per-run forensic traces attached to the bundle regardless of which harness ran the bead.",

.ddx/executions/20260417T182341-5a99310a/manifest.json:13:    "title": "execution-bundle archive: configurable mirror for .ddx/executions/ (including embedded traces)",
.ddx/executions/20260417T182341-5a99310a/manifest.json:14:    "description": "Execution bundles under .ddx/executions/<attempt>/ are the primary forensic record of what DDx automation did, but the most valuable part — the per-iteration agent trace at embedded/agent-*.jsonl — cannot reasonably be checked in. Individual traces can exceed 50 MB; a busy automation day produces hundreds of MB. Axon hit this on 2026-04-15 when GitHub warned about a 50.18 MB embedded trace file, and embedded/ is now gitignored per-repo.

We still want that level of detail for *every* run, for post-mortems, routing/model analysis, and training-data curation. We need a configurable out-of-band mirror.

## Proposed shape

1. **Archive target** configured via `.ddx/config.yaml` (and/or env var):
   - `executions.mirror.kind: local | s3 | gcs | http`
   - `executions.mirror.path: s3://bucket/ddx-executions/{project}/{attempt_id}/` (template with `{project}`, `{attempt_id}`, `{date}`, `{bead_id}`)
   - `executions.mirror.include: [manifest, prompt, result, usage, checks, embedded]` (allow excluding embedded for bandwidth-sensitive setups, but default = everything)
   - `executions.mirror.async: true` (upload in background after execute-bead returns so it never blocks the hot path)

2. **When to mirror**: at execute-bead finalization, after result.json is written. On failure, retry async with bounded attempts; log failures to `.ddx/agent-logs/mirror.log` but do not fail the bead.

3. **Index**: each mirrored bundle gets a pointer row appended to a local `.ddx/executions/mirror-index.jsonl` (attempt_id, bead_id, mirror_uri, uploaded_at, byte_size). This gives analysts a quick `jq` lookup without walking the remote store.

4. **Retrieval**: `ddx agent executions fetch <attempt_id>` pulls a bundle back to .ddx/executions/<attempt_id>/ for local inspection.

5. **GC**: optional local retention policy (`executions.retain_days`) that prunes old local bundles while the mirror keeps the full history.

## Non-goals

- Not a full audit trail service — just a mirror of DDx's existing bundles.
- Not a replacement for git-tracked manifest/prompt/result/usage summaries — those stay checked in.
- Not a distributed log aggregator — one-shot copy per bundle, no streaming.

## Why now

Axon's ADR-018 session produced ~85 MB of execution artifacts in a single day; only 1.5 MB of that is useful as checked-in summary. Without a mirror, every project using DDx execute-bead faces the same tension: either gitignore the traces and lose the forensic data, or commit them and bloat the repo forever.",

.ddx/executions/20260417T171835-e5f1b6a5/prompt.md:17:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260417T171835-e5f1b6a5/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.

Over 2026-04-17 to 2026-04-18, DDx filed five upstream beads in ~/Projects/agent and consumed their fixes:

- agent-92f0f324 (DetectedFlavor) — landed in v0.3.12 ✓
- agent-767549c7 (capability flags) — landed in v0.3.12 ✓
- agent-941e7e42 (AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE) — landed in v0.3.12 ✓ (+ AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL in v0.3.14)
- agent-04639431 (strip thinking field from non-Anthropic) — landed in v0.3.13 ✓
- agent-f237e07b (SSE comment-frame filter) — landed in v0.3.14 ✓
- agent-a365bcf2 (preset rename cheap/default/smart) — OPEN upstream

DDx's ddx-6a5dfe35 (consumer bead for parse failure) was closed 2026-04-18 after v0.3.14 bump landed and omlx drain became viable.

Both fresh-eyes reviews (codex + opus, 2026-04-18) flagged: 'no bead tracks the three upstream fix propagations on the DDx side'. This bead is that tracker.

## Scope

1. Confirm each of the 5 landed upstream beads has a corresponding DDx-side artifact:
   - Version bump commit in DDx go.mod/go.sum — done for v0.3.12, v0.3.13, v0.3.14 (see commits)
   - Any DDx code that needs to consume the new API (e.g., should DDx call Provider.SupportsTools() in its routing?) — cross-reference with ddx-4817edfd (capability gating) which is the consumer bead for agent-767549c7.
   - Any CHANGELOG / release-notes entry in DDx mirroring the upstream fix narrative.

2. Track agent-a365bcf2 (preset rename) through to landing. When upstream ships the rename (smart/default/cheap replacing claude/codex etc.), file a DDx-side bead to migrate any .ddx/config.yaml references or hardcoded preset strings, then this bead can close.

3. File smoke-test bead for each new upstream feature DDx starts consuming:
   - ddx-<v314-smoke> already filed (consumer smoke for omlx SSE path).
   - Consider a bead for exercising AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE in DDx's own eval suite (scripts/eval-skill.sh) to catch integration regressions.

## Non-goals

- Re-implementing the upstream fixes. They've landed.
- Tracking ddx-agent's own backlog (agent-b1c2d3e4 manifest v4, etc.). That's upstream's concern.",

Execution evidence (result.json) already records tokens, cost_usd, duration_ms per attempt. This bead makes those accessible through the CLI:

## Files to touch
- cli/cmd/bead_show.go — add metrics aggregation to JSON output
- cli/cmd/bead_metrics.go — new command
- cli/cmd/bead.go — register metrics subcommand
- cli/cmd/bead_metrics_test.go — test with fixture execution dirs

## Implementation notes
- Scan .ddx/executions/*/result.json files, parse bead_id field, group by bead
- For ddx bead show, only aggregate for the requested bead
- Keep it simple: read from filesystem, no database",

.ddx/executions/20260416T212529-c6fa6346/manifest.json:13:    "description": "## Scope
Add ddx agent metrics tier-success command that aggregates execution outcomes by harness/model tier.

## Context
Execution evidence result.json records harness, concrete model (via manifest.json or result.json), outcome (task_succeeded/task_failed/etc.), cost_usd, and duration_ms. This command aggregates those into tier-level success rates to inform routing policy.

## Files to touch
- cli/cmd/agent_metrics.go — new subcommand under ddx agent metrics
- cli/cmd/agent.go — register metrics subcommand group
- cli/cmd/agent_metrics_test.go — test with fixture execution dirs

## Implementation notes
- Scan .ddx/executions/*/result.json for harness and outcome fields
- Group by harness (or harness+model if useful) 
- Calculate success rate = count(task_succeeded) / count(all)
- Include avg cost and duration per tier",

If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.
